- Logs surface (deferred).
acceptance_criteria: Overview, Journal, Decisions render against fixture DB data seeded by `fixture.seed`, including empty states with concrete onboarding CTA hints, filters, pagination per the contract, row drilldown to detail views, raw JSON visibility, and copy that contains no financial-advice/trade-action language. Browser smoke uses the new browser-test scaffolding.
close_reason: Closed

title: Console Reports and Calibration pages
description:
Implement report-centric Console pages.

Scope:

- Reports page browses deterministic report outputs with active filter, groups, examples, sample warnings, record_ids, truncation state, and raw JSON.
- Calibration page renders Brier, log score, ECE, sharpness, baseline/prevalence, reliability bins, late-recorded exclusions, and embedded integrity diagnostics.
- Every aggregate/chart/drilldown must trace back to `ReportFilter`/`ReportResult` semantics and underlying record IDs.
- Copy must frame metrics as retrospective calibration/process quality, not trading skill or recommendations.
- **Coach report handling:** `report.coach` triggers `signal.scan` (lazy write to signals table). Per `.1` lazy-write list, Console must either (a) render coach via a new side-effect-free read adapter, or (b) explicitly exclude coach from Console with copy that redirects users to the CLI/MCP for coach output. Choose one and document the rationale.

Out of scope:

- New frontend-only canonical analytics that diverge from core reports.
acceptance_criteria: Fixed fixture report envelopes render identically to expected metrics; every chart/group exposes filter + record IDs + raw JSON; low sample/truncation/integrity warnings are visible before users rely on metrics; coach is rendered via a side-effect-free read adapter OR explicitly excluded with documented rationale and a CLI/MCP affordance.
close_reason: Closed

title: Console read-only backend API endpoints
description:
Implement the local Console backend API over the read-only access layer.

Scope:

- Serve typed read endpoints for status, overview, events/journal stream, decisions/detail, instruments, forecasts, outcomes, strategies, playbooks, reports, and raw JSON/debug views. (Logs endpoints are deferred along with the Logs page.)
- Status/health endpoint shape: DB path, schema version, read-only mode, last event timestamp, record counts, lazy-write-handler block status. Used by the Overview page and `tt console serve` startup self-check.
- Keep endpoint semantics aligned with existing `ReportFilter` / `ReportResult` and `record_ids` drilldown contracts (see `src/trade_trace/contracts/report_filter.py`, `src/trade_trace/reports/_filter_support.py`).
- Pagination contract for all list endpoints conforms to the contract defined in the new pagination-and-perf bead (cursor-based recommended).
- **Ban calls to `report.coach`** (and any other handler in the `.1` lazy-write list) from Console endpoint paths. If a coach-style view is needed, build a side-effect-free read adapter; otherwise exclude it with a documented "use the CLI/MCP for this" affordance.
- Avoid write-capable proxying to CLI/MCP tools.
- Avoid POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE for writes. If complex filter POST endpoints are needed, they must be query-only and tested for no DB mutation.
- In-memory caching is acceptable; no persistent cache writes.
- Return helpful errors for missing DB, unsupported schema, and unavailable data.

Out of scope:

- Auth/multi-user/cloud deployment.
- Write endpoints, annotations, imports, outcome recording, memory retain/reflect, playbook update.
- Log endpoints (deferred with Logs page).
acceptance_criteria: Backend endpoint tests cover status/overview/events/decisions/reports/raw-JSON; no write methods or write-capable handlers exist; `ReportFilter`/`ReportResult` parity preserved; DB hash unchanged after endpoint exercise; calls to `report.coach` and other handlers in the `.1` lazy-write list are blocked by code path and asserted by test; pagination contract from the new pagination bead is conformed to on all list endpoints; status/health endpoint reports DB path, schema version, read-only mode, last event timestamp, and record counts.
close_reason: Closed

title: Console frontend shell, navigation, filters, and drilldown grammar
description:
Implement the reusable frontend shell for Trade Trace Console.

Scope:

- Navigation for Overview, Journal, Decisions, Reports, Calibration, Strategies, Playbooks, Evidence & Integrity, and Raw JSON. (Logs nav entry is dropped — Logs page deferred to a follow-up bead outside this epic.)
- Global filter/read context affordances: DB path/status, read-only mode, last-refreshed indicator, manual refresh button, and optional N-second polling toggle per `.1` decision.
- Visible staleness indicator (e.g., "data as of HH:MM:SS").
- Filter state is URL-encoded — every filter combination is deep-linkable, reload-survivable, and shareable.
- Time-zone display per `.1` decision: default UTC, with a user toggle for browser-local time in the global filter bar.
- Drilldown grammar: every rendered `record_id` is clickable and routes to its detail view; aggregate/chart drilldown opens a drawer exposing filter, record IDs, examples, and raw JSON.
- Shared warning/caveat components for sample-size, source-quality, truncation, and unsupported states.
- **No external CDN imports.** All CSS/JS/fonts vendored into the wheel — required for the network-isolation contract owned by the new security bead.
- Visual language: calm observability console, not broker/trading-signal UI.

Out of scope:

- Implementing every page's domain content.
- Logs nav/page (deferred).
acceptance_criteria: Frontend shell renders all top-level routes (Overview, Journal, Decisions, Reports, Calibration, Strategies, Playbooks, Evidence & Integrity, Raw JSON — Logs intentionally absent), displays read-only/non-trading context, supports manual refresh and a visible staleness indicator, provides shared drilldown/raw JSON components, URL-encodes filter state for deep-linking and reload survival, renders timestamps per the `.1` time-zone contract, imports zero external CDN resources, and passes build plus a basic accessibility smoke for navigation/focus.
close_reason: Closed

title: Console architecture and read-only threat model
description:
Define the architecture contract for Trade Trace Console before implementation. This bead must produce a concrete decision document, not an open question list.

Required decisions (must be named explicitly in the architecture note):

- CLI grammar: `tt console serve` (conform to existing `subject verb` pattern; see `src/trade_trace/core.py:46` `build_registry()`).
- Default browser-open behavior on `tt console serve` (open / do not open / `--browser` flag), and the `--no-browser` opt-out.
- Backend stack: pick one of FastAPI / Starlette / Flask. Document the rationale.
- Frontend stack and build pipeline: pick one of Vite + React / Vue / htmx / etc. Document the build output path under `src/trade_trace/console/static/`.
- Dependency strategy: base package vs `[console]` extra. Recommend `[console]` extra to keep core install lean (matches existing `mcp` and `embeddings` extras).
- SQLite read-only mechanism: `sqlite3.connect("file:<path>?mode=ro", uri=True)` (URI mode) so OS-level read-only is enforced, not just call-site discipline.
- Enumerated lazy-write handler list. Known seed: `report.coach` triggers `signal.scan` (appends signal rows) in `src/trade_trace/tools/signals.py`. The architecture note must list every handler in `src/trade_trace/reports/` and `src/trade_trace/tools/` flagged as side-effect-risky, and decide for each whether Console exposes it (via a side-effect-free read adapter) or excludes it.
- Sensitive-data redaction posture for journal content rendered in HTML: minimum policy is truncate long strings, strip terminal escape sequences, no opaque base64/binary inlined, and explicit allowlist for `record_id` / `decision_id` / `subject` field rendering.
- Time-zone display contract: default UTC, with a user-toggle to render in browser-local time. Decide where the toggle lives in the global filter bar.
- Refresh-cadence contract: manual refresh button is mandatory; optional N-second polling default decided here; visible staleness indicator ("data as of HH:MM:SS").
- Browser test framework recommendation (handed off to the new browser-test-scaffolding bead). Recommend Playwright unless a strong alternative is documented.

Scope:

- Confirm `tt console serve` launch shape and local-server default bind/port behavior; non-loopback bind requires `--allow-non-loopback` with a stderr warning.
- Specify backend endpoint families and which ones are query-only.
- Define the read-only threat model: no migrations, no event/outbox/signal/recall/telemetry writes, no persistent preference writes into the journal DB.
- Formally scope the **Logs page out of MVP** (a follow-up operational-logging bead and a follow-up Console-Logs-page bead are filed outside this epic).

Out of scope:

- Implementing server/frontend code.
- Choosing visual polish beyond the core UI grammar.
- Building the operational-logging infrastructure that a future Logs page would consume.

Notes:

This bead is the first executable leaf for the epic. It must leave later implementation beads with a stable contract and a named stack — not open product questions.
acceptance_criteria: Architecture note produced as a written deliverable that names: CLI grammar (`tt console serve`), browser-open default, backend stack, frontend stack and build pipeline, dependency strategy (base vs `[console]` extra), SQLite read-only mechanism (`?mode=ro` URI), enumerated lazy-write handler list (with `report.coach`/`signal.scan` listed), sensitive-data redaction posture, time-zone display contract, refresh cadence and staleness indicator, browser-test framework recommendation, and formal Logs-page deferral. No implementation starts until this contract is accepted or explicitly narrowed.
close_reason: Closed

title: Console packaging, CLI entrypoint, and dependency strategy
description:
Implement the package/CLI foundation for launching Trade Trace Console from the PyPI package.

Scope:

- Add `tt console serve` / `trade-trace console serve` entrypoint, registered in `src/trade_trace/core.py` `build_registry()` to match the existing `subject verb` grammar.
- Implement `--host` (default `127.0.0.1`), `--port` (default chosen in `.1`), `--no-browser`, and `--allow-non-loopback` flags. Non-loopback binds emit a stderr warning before serving.
- Port-in-use handling: print a helpful error naming the port, suggest an alternate port, exit with a documented non-zero code (no traceback).
- Ctrl+C / SIGTERM: graceful shutdown without traceback.
- Print startup information: local URL, DB path, log path/status (or `logs deferred` note while the Logs page is out of MVP), read-only mode, and non-trading/no-fetching notice.
- Decide and implement base-vs-`[console]` dependency behavior per the `.1` decision.
- Package compiled frontend assets into the Python wheel. The wheel build must fail (or warn loudly) if frontend assets are missing from `src/trade_trace/console/static/`. Static-asset bundling pattern documented in `pyproject.toml` and `MANIFEST.in`.

Out of scope:

- Building all dashboard pages.
- Adding remote/cloud hosting or auth.
- Auto-opening the user's browser unless `.1` decided this is the default.

Acceptance criteria are tightened separately.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh install exposes `tt console serve --help` and starts a local server. Wheel includes required static assets and the build fails if they are absent. `--host` / `--port` / `--no-browser` / `--allow-non-loopback` flags exist; localhost default enforced; non-loopback emits explicit stderr warning. Port-in-use produces a helpful non-traceback error with documented exit code. Ctrl+C / SIGTERM produces graceful shutdown without traceback. Startup banner reports URL, DB path, read-only mode, non-trading notice, and Logs-deferred status. Missing `[console]` dependencies (if extra is used) fail with a helpful message.
close_reason: Closed

title: Read-only SQLite access layer for Console
description:
Build the Console data-access substrate for live reads from the journal SQLite DB.

Scope:

- Add `open_database_readonly(path)` helper in `src/trade_trace/storage/database.py` that opens with the `sqlite3.connect("file:<path>?mode=ro", uri=True)` mechanism decided in `.1`. OS-level read-only must be enforced, not just call-site discipline.
- Provide safe query helpers for journal overview, events, records, report backing data, and drilldowns. All helpers go through the read-only handle.
- Handle missing DB, empty DB, and unsupported schema gracefully with helpful error messages (no crashes).
- Ensure Console access paths do not trigger migrations or write-side effects (cross-reference the lazy-write list produced by `.1`).
- Tests must assert DB file hash is unchanged after representative read patterns AND that the read-only handle rejects `INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DDL` at the SQLite layer.

**Removed from scope (deferred):** log discovery / tail / parsing helpers. The Logs page is out of MVP per the epic-review decision; an operational-logging contract follow-up bead and a Console-Logs-page follow-up bead are filed outside this epic. This bead is DB-access-only.

Out of scope:

- Full UI rendering.
- Log helpers (moved to follow-up bead outside this epic).
- External log collectors or telemetry.
acceptance_criteria: `open_database_readonly(path)` exists in `src/trade_trace/storage/database.py` and uses `sqlite3.connect("file:<path>?mode=ro", uri=True)`. Tests prove DB file hash unchanged after representative read patterns AND that the read-only handle rejects `INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DDL` at the SQLite layer. Missing DB, empty DB, and unsupported schema produce graceful non-crashing states. No log helpers are added by this bead (deferred).
close_reason: Closed

title: Trade Trace Console read-only local dashboard
description:
# Trade Trace Console — read-only local dashboard epic

## Purpose

Create **Trade Trace Console**, a local, package-shipped, read-only web console for end users to review their AI trading agent's Trade Trace journal from the local SQLite database and diagnostic logs.

The console is an observability/review surface, not a trading surface. It should help users understand what their agent recorded, what decisions and forecasts resolved, how calibration is trending, what evidence was captured, which strategies/playbooks recur, and what records need retrospective review.

## User-approved direction

- Product name: **Trade Trace Console**.
- Delivery shape: local live server shipped with the PyPI package.
- Audience: end users reviewing their AI agent's journal.
- Data source: live reads from the Trade Trace SQLite database plus configured local logs.
- Scope: plan for the full simple dashboard rather than a review-packet-first MVP.
- Boundary: fully read-only; no annotations, edits, comments, or journal mutations.

## Capability contract

Console MUST preserve Trade Trace's existing safety/product boundaries:

- Localhost-only by default.
- SQLite journal DB remains the source of truth.
- Logs are secondary diagnostic context, not canonical ledger state.
- No trade execution.
- No broker/wallet credentials.
- No market-data fetching.
- No external telemetry or outbound network calls during normal use.
- No write-capable journal APIs exposed from Console.
- No comments, labels, notes, annotations, or preferences written into the journal DB.
- No hidden frontend-only canonical metrics that diverge from CLI/MCP reports.
- No financial-advice framing, signals, or trade recommendations.

## Architecture direction

Preferred architecture:

- Add a `tt console` / `trade-trace console` command.
- Serve a local HTTP UI bound to `127.0.0.1` by default.
- Bundle compiled frontend assets in the Python wheel.
- Keep console dependencies lean; consider a `[console]` extra first if web stack dependencies are non-trivial.
- Backend endpoints are typed, read-only views over existing DB/report contracts.
- Prefer polling/manual refresh for the initial live dashboard; defer SSE/websockets unless needed.
- Open SQLite in read-only mode where practical.
- Avoid running migrations from Console.
- Avoid calling read-like handlers that have write side effects (for example telemetry, recall events, or lazy signal writes) unless an explicit side-effect-free Console path exists.

## Initial page map

1. **Overview** — DB path, schema/version, read-only mode, last event timestamp, log status, record counts, and high-priority data-quality warnings.
2. **Journal** — chronological event/record stream filtered by time, event type, actor, agent/model/environment/run, subject kind/id.
3. **Decisions** — decision table and detail drawer with thesis, forecast, snapshot, sources, outcome, score, reflections, and playbook adherence.
4. **Reports** — deterministic report browser with filter, groups, sample warnings, examples, record IDs, and raw JSON.
5. **Calibration** — Brier/log/ECE/sharpness/baseline/reliability bins plus integrity diagnostics and drilldown.
6. **Strategies** — strategy list/details with decision/thesis/forecast counts, calibration/P&L summaries where available, tags, reflections, and playbook slices.
7. **Playbooks** — playbook/version adherence counts, overrides, outcomes, rule provenance, and linked decisions.
8. **Evidence & Integrity** — source-quality and calibration-integrity diagnostics.
9. **Logs** — configured local log excerpts with filtering and safe handling of missing/corrupt logs.
10. **Raw JSON** — report envelopes, filters, selected DB records, and endpoint responses for trust/debuggability.

## Functional requirements

- `tt console` starts a local web UI for a configured/current Trade Trace journal.
- Console displays a clear local/read-only/non-trading notice on startup and in the UI.
- Console reads live DB/log state and refreshes without restart.
- Every aggregate/report chart exposes the underlying report/filter/raw JSON and drilldown record IDs where available.
- Overview surfaces data-quality caveats before performance-style metrics.
- Decision review treats skips, watches, holds, thesis updates, reviews, paper decisions, and actual-recorded decisions as first-class journal records.
- Logs page tolerates missing logs and malformed/corrupt lines.
- Empty DB state is useful and does not look broken.
- Binding to `0.0.0.0` or other non-loopback interfaces is explicit opt-in with a warning.

## Read-only requirements

Read-only must be enforceable, not just a UI convention:

- No `POST`/`PUT`/`PATCH`/`DELETE` write endpoints in MVP.
- If complex filters require `POST`, endpoints must be query-only and covered by DB-unchanged tests.
- Console must not run migrations automatically.
- Console must not append events, outbox rows, recall events, signals, or preferences.
- Console must not call write tools or write-capable MCP/CLI proxy surfaces.
- In-memory caching is acceptable; persistent cache writes are deferred unless separately designed outside the journal DB.

## Verification contract

Implementation is not complete until these checks pass:

- Fresh package install can launch Console (`tt console --help`, then server start).
- Server binds to `127.0.0.1` by default and reports URL, DB path, log path/status, and read-only mode.
- Fixture DB browser smoke covers Overview, Journal, Decisions, Reports, Calibration, Evidence/Integrity, Logs, and Raw JSON.
- DB file hash is unchanged after exercising Console pages.
- Tests prove no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DDL/migration is executed by Console paths.
- Tests block outbound sockets and normal Console usage still works.
- Missing DB, empty DB, old/unsupported schema, missing logs, and malformed logs produce helpful non-crashing states.
- Report panels match existing report envelopes for fixed fixtures.
- Drilldown record IDs/filters match `ReportResult` semantics.
- Frontend build/static assets are included in the wheel.
- Accessibility smoke covers keyboard navigation, focus visibility, contrast, and no hover-only critical information.

## Non-goals / deferred

- Trade execution or order controls.
- Broker, wallet, or market-data integrations.
- Cloud dashboard, sync, authentication, or multi-user SaaS.
- User comments, annotations, or labels.
- Review-packet-specific workflow as the first slice.
- Background workers, alerts, notifications, or scheduler.
- Embeddings/model management UI.
- Live websocket/SSE stream unless polling proves insufficient.

## Open decisions for child planning

- Whether Console dependencies ship in the base package or `[console]` extra.
- Exact Python web stack.
- Exact frontend stack/build pipeline.
- Log discovery/configuration contract.
- Which existing report handlers are side-effect-free enough for Console and which need side-effect-free read adapters.

## Suggested child workstreams

When this epic is broken down, use task-level beads for:

1. Console architecture/API contract and read-only threat model.
2. Packaging/CLI entrypoint and dependency strategy.
3. Read-only DB/log access layer.
4. Console backend endpoints.
5. Frontend shell/navigation/filtering/drilldown grammar.
6. Overview/Journal/Decisions pages.
7. Reports/Calibration pages.
8. Strategies/Playbooks pages.
9. Evidence/Integrity/Logs/Raw JSON pages.
10. End-to-end verification, wheel install smoke, no-write/no-network tests, and docs update.

## Materialized child graph

Materialized child graph on 2026-05-19:

- trade-trace-1kkv.1 — Console architecture and read-only threat model (first executable leaf)
- trade-trace-1kkv.2 — Console packaging, CLI entrypoint, and dependency strategy
- trade-trace-1kkv.3 — Read-only SQLite and log access layer for Console
- trade-trace-1kkv.4 — Console read-only backend API endpoints
- trade-trace-1kkv.5 — Console frontend shell, navigation, filters, and drilldown grammar
- trade-trace-1kkv.6 — Console Overview, Journal, and Decisions pages
- trade-trace-1kkv.7 — Console Reports and Calibration pages
- trade-trace-1kkv.8 — Console Strategies and Playbooks pages
- trade-trace-1kkv.9 — Console Evidence, Integrity, Logs, and Raw JSON pages
- trade-trace-1kkv.10 — Console UX and product-boundary review gate
- trade-trace-1kkv.11 — Console user docs and safety copy update
- trade-trace-1kkv.12 — Console final verification and release-readiness gate

Execution model: parent-child links make this epic the canonical container. Task dependencies make trade-trace-1kkv.1 the first ready leaf. The final verification gate trade-trace-1kkv.12 is blocked by all material implementation/QC/docs children and must close before the root epic closes.
acceptance_criteria: Epic is ready to close only after child work implements a package-shipped local Console with read-only DB/log access, no journal mutations, no outbound network during normal use, report/drilldown parity with existing envelopes, wheel install smoke coverage, browser smoke coverage, and updated user docs.
notes:
Materialized child graph on 2026-05-19:

- trade-trace-1kkv.1 — Console architecture and read-only threat model (first executable leaf)
- trade-trace-1kkv.2 — Console packaging, CLI entrypoint, and dependency strategy
- trade-trace-1kkv.3 — Read-only SQLite and log access layer for Console
- trade-trace-1kkv.4 — Console read-only backend API endpoints
- trade-trace-1kkv.5 — Console frontend shell, navigation, filters, and drilldown grammar
- trade-trace-1kkv.6 — Console Overview, Journal, and Decisions pages
- trade-trace-1kkv.7 — Console Reports and Calibration pages
- trade-trace-1kkv.8 — Console Strategies and Playbooks pages
- trade-trace-1kkv.9 — Console Evidence, Integrity, Logs, and Raw JSON pages
- trade-trace-1kkv.10 — Console UX and product-boundary review gate
- trade-trace-1kkv.11 — Console user docs and safety copy update
- trade-trace-1kkv.12 — Console final verification and release-readiness gate

Execution model: parent-child links make this epic the canonical container. Task dependencies make trade-trace-1kkv.1 the first ready leaf. The final verification gate trade-trace-1kkv.12 is blocked by all material implementation/QC/docs children and must close before the root epic closes.
Rescope on 2026-05-19 after review:

- Logs page DEFERRED from MVP. Codebase has no logging infrastructure today (no centralized logger, no agent log files, stderr reserved for MCP).
- `.9` renamed to "Console Evidence, Integrity, and Raw JSON pages" (Logs scope removed).
- `.3` renamed to "Read-only SQLite access layer for Console" (log helpers removed).
- CLI grammar normalized to `tt console serve` (conform to existing `subject verb` pattern).
- 3 new child beads added: `.13` security/CSP/network-isolation, `.14` pagination/perf baseline, `.15` browser-test scaffolding.
- 2 follow-up beads filed OUTSIDE this epic: trade-trace-3zvl (operational logging contract) and trade-trace-jtec (Console Logs page follow-up; depends on 3zvl and waits for this epic to close).
- PRD/README/VISION divergence (README documents "no human dashboard / P2 static exports") to be reconciled in `.11` as part of docs work.
- All existing child beads had description/acceptance tightened: `.1` now produces a concrete decision document (stack picks, redaction posture, time zone, refresh cadence, lazy-write handler list); `.2` adds port-in-use / Ctrl+C / flags; `.4` bans `report.coach` along Console paths; `.5` mandates URL-encoded filter state and no-CDN imports; `.6` adds empty-DB onboarding CTA; `.11` adds PRD/README/VISION reconciliation; `.12` adds CSP / no-network / lazy-write-block / pagination-baseline / redaction proofs.

Current child count: 15 (was 12).
close_reason: All 15 children landed; release-gate proof in docs/architecture/console-release-gate.md (trade-trace-1kkv.12). Logs page deferred to follow-up trade-trace-jtec.

title: Stand up and validate local launch
description:
Session task: stand up Trade Trace locally and run end-to-end validation.

Scope:

- Run full repository tests with isolated environment variables.

- Full pytest suite result is recorded.
- CLI journal loop is exercised through persisted DB state and reporting.
- MCP stdio/tool path is verified or a concrete blocker is recorded.
- Any remaining launch caveats are explicit.
notes:

- CLI E2E smoke passed with isolated `/tmp/trade-trace-standup-home`: schema 10, outbound_network_active false, embeddings none, 1 venue/instrument/snapshot/thesis/forecast/decision/outcome, 1 forecast_scores row, Brier score 0.2304, pending_count 0, calibration sample_size 1.
- MCP stdio console-script smoke passed with isolated `/tmp/trade-trace-standup-mcp-home`: initialize handshake, 66 tools listed, journal.init/status via tools/call, venue.add persisted a row.
- Independent review subagent inspected the uncommitted static-gate cleanup diff and found no critical issues or test-shaped weakening.

Code changes made:
- Safe lint/type cleanup across source/tests: import ordering, exception chaining, explicit zip(strict=False), Python 3.11 datetime.UTC, mypy narrowing/casts/assertions, jsonschema import ignore, and test typing annotations.

Remaining caveats:

title: Cover explicit metadata_json credential injection in no-credentials policy
description:
Context:
Domain: security-boundaries
Candidate: DEBT-036
Raw source: lane:7#SEC-02
Artifacts: audits/no-tech-debt-20260518/lane-reports/lane-7.md

Technical-debt claim:
Cover explicit metadata_json credential injection in no-credentials policy

Evidence:
tests pass credential-shaped unknown top-level args, but _store_metadata_json accepts caller-provided JSON/string unchanged; metadata_json={api_key:...} is not tested.

## Steps to Reproduce
Pass credential-shaped values inside explicit `metadata_json`, including nested object or raw JSON string, through write tools covered by no-credentials policy; current `_store_metadata_json` returns/stores caller JSON unchanged.

Carrying cost / risk:
No-credential guarantee can be bypassed via metadata_json and exported in events/JSONL.

Target paydown:
Decide reject/sanitize/document metadata_json credentials; add nested/string metadata_json tests.

Non-goals / boundaries:
- No broad rewrite; no unrelated behavior/product changes; preserve documented local/offline security boundaries unless this row explicitly revises docs.

Behavior/product boundary:
Pay down the named maintenance debt only; product feature expansion is out of scope unless routed as design/investigation.

Routing / classification:
- remediation_track: bug
- debt_class: security-hardening, test-debt
- priority: P1
- risk: high
- confidence: high
- justification: Track=bug; disposition=accept; belongs here when accepted because it has evidence, carrying cost, bounded paydown, and validation path. Sibling workflow routing is recorded where track is bug/deadcode/simplification.

Risks / intentional-debt check:

projections.py realized_pnl += (price - avg_entry_price) * qty_delta; long open +100 close -100 at profit yields negative P&L under tests’ convention.

title: Make memory.reflect node + about-edge write truly atomic
description:
Context:

Raw source: lane:4#DTLMP-001
Artifacts: audits/no-tech-debt-20260518/lane-reports/lane-4.md

Technical-debt claim:
Make memory.reflect node + about-edge write truly atomic

Evidence:
memory.reflect docstring/docs claim one atomic operation, but implementation calls _memory_retain in one UnitOfWork then inserts about edge in a second UnitOfWork.

Create a memory.reflect call where node retention succeeds and about-edge insertion/event emission fails; current implementation uses two UnitOfWork transactions so the reflection node can commit without the about edge.

reports-memory-playbook-strategy / src/trade_trace/reports/calibration.py, src/trade_trace/reports/tag_aggregates.py, src/trade_trace/reports/watchlist.py, src/trade_trace/reports/unscored.py, src/trade_trace/reports/playbook_adherence.py, src/trade_trace/contracts/report_filter.py

Observed behavior:
Reports accept/echo filters but compute over global or over-broad rows.

Expected behavior:
Apply supported filters or reject unsupported filter fields; never echo ignored filters as applied.

Evidence:
primary_evidence.txt: calibration.py validates ReportFilter then calls _load_scored_rows(conn) globally; delegate found same pattern in tag_aggregates/watchlist/unscored/playbook_adherence.

Failure mode / impact:
Agents can make strategy/time/instrument decisions from falsely scoped reports.

## Steps to Reproduce
Seed mixed data and run report.calibration/report.playbook_adherence with filters; metrics/record_ids must exclude non-matching rows.

Duplicate check:
Compared against existing open Beads and the exhaustive bughunt candidate matrix after `bd find-duplicates` preflight. Not a duplicate because this candidate has a distinct root cause/failure mode/fix surface. Possible overlap: none.

Implement the smallest fix that makes observed behavior match the stated contract. For static-only candidates, first add a failure-injection or targeted regression test proving the risk.

Validation:
Seed mixed data and run report.calibration/report.playbook_adherence with filters; metrics/record_ids must exclude non-matching rows.

- `sources.redaction_status='sensitive'` sources are omitted with caveat counts; `redacted` sources omit body/extracted_text/excerpt/summary/note while preserving metadata.

title: Implement import.validate/import.commit JSONL replay path
description:
Context:
JSONL import is documented as the P1 replay path but current tools are contract stubs.

Evidence:
- README.md:48-49 says import.validate/import.commit are contract stubs and P1 implementation is pending.
- docs/architecture/imports.md:5-11 says P1 ships actual import command path.
- src/trade_trace/tools/imports.py handlers raise UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY.

Scope:
Implement validation and commit for JSONL files/directories emitted by the exporter, using core dispatch and respecting transport metadata stripping, id strategy, idempotency, and transaction mode.
acceptance_criteria:
- `import.validate` parses file and directory inputs, validates line shape/tool existence/schema/id strategy, reports errors without writes.
- `import.commit` replays valid lines through core dispatch with documented transaction modes and rollback behavior.
- Exporter-output replay ignores underscore transport metadata.
- Tests cover validate no-write, atomic rollback, per-row partial progress, idempotent replay, ID-strategy conflict, and forward-reference rejection.
notes: Implemented import.validate/import.commit JSONL replay path. import.validate now always uses isolated staged home and performs no writes, including omitted-home/default-home calls. import.commit supports per_row partial progress and single-mode copy-on-write DB staging/replacement for atomic all-or-nothing commits; exporter underscore metadata is stripped; import-ready tool allowlist blocks recursive/read tools; tests cover no-write validation, runtime-failure rollback, per-row progress, idempotent replay, ID-strategy conflict, and forward-reference rejection. Validation: targeted import/MCP/security suite -> 85 passed; full pytest -> 961 passed, 1 skipped (dogfood opt-in).
close_reason: Fixed

title: EPIC: Agent-ready — stdio MCP server, input schemas, agent guide, embeddings
description:
Post-MVP epic to make trade-trace consumable by external MCP-speaking LLM agents.

The trade-trace-1mx MVP epic closed with 834 tests but every path uses the in-process mcp_call shim. The actual stdio MCP server is a NotImplementedError stub (src/trade_trace/mcp_server.py:54-66). Without this, Claude Desktop / Claude Code / Cursor / etc. literally cannot connect.

This epic closes that gap end-to-end:
- stdio MCP server (mcp SDK bridge to ToolRegistry).
- Auto-derived per-tool inputSchema from example_minimal payloads.
- AGENT_GUIDE.md walkthrough of the journal loop.
- Client config docs for Claude Code, Cursor/Windsurf/Cline, Claude Desktop.
- CI smoke test (subprocess + tools/list + tools/call round-trip).
- Embeddings opt-in (sqlite-vec + bge-small + OS keyring + tt memory reindex) — pulled forward from previously-deferred trade-trace-a4p.
- Living dogfood-findings tracker bead.
- Test QC + Docs QC + Gate.

User decisions captured in plan-mode Q&A 2026-05-18 (plan file: /home/hermes/.claude/plans/composed-riding-whale.md):
- Support all 3 host families (Claude Code, IDE-generic, Claude Desktop).
- Auto-derive inputSchema from example_minimal (faster than per-tool Pydantic models; can upgrade later).
- Include the embeddings opt-in in this epic, dogfood-tracker bead, and CI smoke test.

Done definition: a user can pip install trade-trace[mcp], add the MCP server to a host config, restart the host, see ~40 tools via tools/list each with a valid inputSchema, and drive the full PRD §10 loop from natural-language prompts. Optionally enable semantic recall via tt config set embeddings.provider local.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Stdio MCP server is shippable through a real host config, not only the in-process shim.
- tools/list exposes every registered tool with valid inputSchema.
- Agent guide and client config docs are truthful against implementation.
- Embeddings opt-in paths are implemented and off-by-default safety is proven.
- Test QC, docs QC, and final gate beads close with direct command/file evidence.
notes: Agent-ready epic closure sweep: parent-scoped ready/open/in_progress/blocked leaf queries are empty. Final gate trade-trace-dhs closed with direct evidence: mcp+dev venv stdio/schema suite 13 passed, base full pytest 1040 passed/6 skipped, embeddings/admin/keyring suite 43 passed, docs linked and verified, dogfood tracker trade-trace-4ng remains a living/deferred notes bead related to this epic and not closed. The only remaining linked dogfood bead is intentionally deferred for future successor-epic consumption, not blocking this epic's shipped acceptance criteria.
close_reason: fixed

title: Final Verification: Trade Trace MVP dogfood scenario and contract audit
description:
Prove the MVP learning loop is implemented and useful enough to close the root epic.

## Scope
In scope:
- Run the full deterministic test suite and lint/type checks.
- Run or create an end-to-end dogfood/smoke scenario aligned to PRD §10: journal.init, decisions/forecasts/outcomes, scoring, reports/coach, reflections, playbook update, later adherence, recall during later thesis.
- Verify zero trade execution paths, zero broker/wallet credentials, zero default outbound network, CLI/MCP parity, docs truthfulness, and P1/P2 deferral accuracy.

Out of scope:
- Shipping deferred P1/P2 features just to satisfy final verification.
- Any public/remote publication unless separately authorized.

## Known leads
- PRD §10 MVP definition of done
- README MVP vertical slice
- Subagent review recommended deterministic dogfood protocol and final contract audit.

## Validation
- python -m ruff check .
- python -m ruff format --check .
- python -m mypy src tests
- python -m pytest
- trade-trace journal init --home /tmp/trade-trace-smoke
- trade-trace journal status --home /tmp/trade-trace-smoke
- trade-trace report filter_schema --home /tmp/trade-trace-smoke

## Decision readiness
human-decision-readiness: afk_capable
resolved decisions:
- Product boundary from README/VISION/PRD: local AI-only journal/memory/calibration substrate; no trade execution; no broker/wallet credentials; no external trading-data fetching.
- MVP scope is PRD M0-M4 only; P1/P2 implementations remain out of scope except contract stubs/import-ready schemas where PRD explicitly requires them.
- Vectors/embedding network paths are off by default; any embedding provider/model download requires explicit opt-in and must not transmit trading data by default.
- CLI and MCP share one core API; parity is semantic after transport normalization, not byte-identical.
defaulted decisions:
- decision: Dogfood proof can start as deterministic synthetic/local fixture before 30 real calendar days of trading data.; default: Use synthetic/local fixture for implementation acceptance; real 30-day dogfood remains a later product-validation follow-up if desired.; rationale: The repo is fresh and the implementation epic must be closable without waiting a calendar month; fixture proves mechanics while PRD can separately track long-run dogfood.; reversal_cost: low
human decisions required:
- none
credential/external access needs:
- none

## Convergence targets
- gate-m4-playbook
- define-dogfood-protocol
- synthesize-contract-decisions
- gate-m0-foundation
- gate-m1-ledger
- gate-m2-reports
- gate-m3-memory
- test-m0-foundation
- docs-m0-foundation
- test-m1-ledger
- docs-m1-ledger
- test-m2-reports
- docs-m2-reports
- test-m3-memory-strategy
- docs-m3-memory-strategy
- test-m4-playbook
- docs-m4-playbook

## Acceptance Criteria
- All phase gates are closed with evidence or explicitly deferred/superseded with notes.
- PRD §10.1 plumbing criteria are demonstrated in tests/smoke output.
- PRD §10.2 loop-useful criteria are either mechanically demonstrated or explicitly classified as longer dogfood follow-up with a new bead.
- No implementation or docs claim Trade Trace executes trades, fetches trading data, handles broker/wallet credentials, or has default network behavior.
- README/PRD/docs status reflects implemented vs planned surfaces.

## Executor notes
- Do not close this bead from phase-gate prose alone; rerun the strongest practical checks in the final session.

## Graph evolution rules
- Any material final-verification failure must become a blocking fix bead related to the epic before this bead closes.
## Validation expectations
- Rerun the strongest practical full-suite checks available in the final session.
- Inspect live Beads graph/readiness and ensure no material follow-up remains unwired.
- Confirm docs truthfulness after implementation, not only tests.
acceptance_criteria:
Re-run full test suite + dogfood fixture from trade-trace-8dv in the FINAL session. Prose-only close reasons are not sufficient — every acceptance bullet below produces a runnable assertion against the dogfood fixture.

PRD §10.1 Plumbing (9 criteria — each is its own assertion):
(1) Initialize journal: `tt journal init` succeeds twice (idempotence).
(2) Record ≥30 decisions: fixture has decisions table count >= 30.
(3) Resolve ≥5 supported binary forecasts: forecast_scores table count >= 5 + outcomes.status='resolved_final' on linked rows.
(4) Run deterministic reports and report.coach: all 7 reports + coach return success envelopes with bit-identical output across 3 runs (SHA-256).
(5) Write ≥10 reflections: memory_nodes WHERE node_type='reflection' count >= 10, each with ≥1 edge.
(6) Propose ≥1 playbook version with provenance from reflection: playbook_versions WHERE provenance_reflection_node_id IS NOT NULL count >= 1.
(7) Record decision_playbook_rules adherence on later decisions: decision_playbook_rules count >= 5; report.playbook_adherence surfaces row counts.
(8) Recall relevant prior memories during later thesis: memory.recall in fixture trace returns >=1 row with strategy_provenance.
(9) Execute zero trades / zero execution credentials: trade-trace-r0v grep audit passes (zero forbidden patterns).

PRD §10.2 Loop-useful (7 criteria):
(10) ≥1 report identifies recurring error/strength agent didn't preempt: report.mistakes (or strengths) returns ≥1 tag with count >=3 not present in any pre-decision thesis.
(11) ≥1 memory.recall result cited in later thesis traceable via derived_from or supports edge: edges table has matching entry.
(12) ≥1 playbook rule changes later decision (followed | overridden): decision_playbook_rules where status in (followed, overridden) count >= 1, with linked outcome captured.
(13) ≥1 ambiguous-resolution case handled: outcomes where status in (ambiguous, disputed, resolved_provisional) count >= 1, linked forecast stayed pending.
(14) Calibration report states sample size + emits sample_warning when N<20: assertion on report.calibration output meta.sample_warning string.
(15) ≥1 strategy-scoped recall surfaces uncited memory traceable via derived_from or supports edge: edges + memory_recall_events confirmed.
(16) Calibration report surfaces sharpness signal distinguishing "always 50%" from confident-calibrated: numeric sharpness in output non-zero for confident forecaster; zero for always-50% forecaster.

Final-verification close conditions:
- ALL 16 assertions pass in the same session.
- Hardening beads (8dv, 4qf, bwv, b2r, 268, r0v, 89f, 64q, jzn, l9q, aa2, bwo) closed.
- All 4 QC gates (744, xtd, xzt, e3p, v8i) closed with evidence.
- Final docs truthfulness (89f) and boundary audit (r0v) closed.
- Close reason includes: test names + counts per criterion + SHA-256 hashes proving determinism.
notes:
Final verification scope expanded by planning hardening pass.

Before closing final verification, verify these added blockers are closed, deferred with notes, or superseded:
- trade-trace-bwo: M1: Implement import-ready JSONL schema/envelope contract stubs
- trade-trace-aa2: M3: Reconcile memory implementation plan with PRD off-default embeddings and playbook_rule taxonomy
- trade-trace-4qf: MVP Security: Threat model, secret scanning, and redaction audit for local journal data
- trade-trace-8dv: MVP Eval Harness: Build deterministic dogfood fixture dataset for loop-usefulness tests
- trade-trace-bwv: MVP Operability: Migration, backup, restore, and projection-rebuild safety drill
- trade-trace-b2r: MVP Operability: JSONL export shape and replay-readiness validator
- trade-trace-l9q: MVP Provenance: Source quality rules and evidence coverage report
- trade-trace-jzn: MVP Calibration Integrity: Anti-goodhart diagnostics for forecast coverage and resolution hygiene
- trade-trace-64q: MVP Reproducibility: Report and recall run metadata for deterministic replay
- trade-trace-268: MVP Ergonomics: Agent-facing schema examples, dry-run validation, and next-action hints
- trade-trace-89f: Final Docs Truthfulness: shipped MVP vs contract-only vs deferred surfaces
- trade-trace-r0v: MVP Boundary Audit: P1/P2 negative-scope and absent-surface verification

Additional final-verification expectations:
- negative-scope audit proves no accidental trade execution, broker/wallet credential, market/outcome fetch, telemetry, web dashboard, sync, full import, risk/opportunity/compare, or review.bundle implementation surface shipped as MVP unless explicitly unsupported/deferred;
- docs-wide truthfulness distinguishes shipped MVP, contract-only stubs, optional/off-default capabilities, and deferred P1/P2 work;
- root epic appearing in `bd ready` is treated as harmless grouping noise, not executable work.
close_reason:
FINAL VERIFICATION PASS. All 16 PRD §10 dogfood criteria assert against the deterministic journal.fixture_seed --target=mvp-eval fixture (bead 8dv).

PRD §10.1 Plumbing (9 criteria) — tests in tests/integration/test_final_dogfood_verification.py:
(1) test_p1_initialize_journal_idempotent — journal.init succeeds twice on the same home; schema_version matches.
(2) test_p2_record_at_least_30_decisions — fixture decisions count == 30.
(3) test_p3_resolve_at_least_five_binary_forecasts — forecast_scores count >= 5; outcomes resolved_final count >= 5.
(4) test_p4_reports_and_coach_return_success — 11 shipped reports + coach all return ok=true on the fixture.
(5) test_p5_at_least_10_reflections_each_with_an_edge — 10 reflection nodes; orphan invariant (zero reflections without an about-edge) holds.
(6) test_p6_playbook_version_with_reflection_provenance — provenance_reflection_node_id non-null + linked node is node_type='reflection'.
(7) test_p7_adherence_rows_present_and_reportable — ≥5 adherence rows; report.playbook_adherence surfaces the count.
(8) test_p8_recall_returns_strategy_provenance — memory.recall returns items each carrying strategy_provenance.
(9) test_p9_zero_trades_zero_execution_credentials — no column in any shipped table matches wallet|broker|seed|signing|private_key|api_key.

PRD §10.2 Loop-useful (7 criteria):
(10) test_l10_report_identifies_recurring_pattern — report.mistakes returns ≥1 tag with decision_count >= 3 (fixture seeds repeated 'liquidity-ignored' tag across decisions).
(11) test_l11_recall_cited_in_later_thesis_via_supports_edge — ≥1 thesis carries derived_from or supports edge to a memory_node (fixture seeds two such edges).
(12) test_l12_playbook_rule_changes_later_decision_with_outcome — ≥1 followed/overridden adherence row on a decision whose instrument has a recorded outcome.
(13) test_l13_ambiguous_resolution_case_handled — outcomes in {ambiguous, disputed, resolved_provisional} count >= 1; pending forecasts on those instruments stay pending (no autoscore).
(14) test_l14_calibration_sample_warning_below_threshold — meta.sample_warning carries '20' (fixture seeds 5 scored forecasts, below default min_sample=20).
(15) test_l15_strategy_scoped_recall_traceable_via_edge — memory.recall(context=strategy) surfaces a memory_node with an inbound edge from a thesis carrying the matching strategy_id.
(16) test_l16_calibration_sharpness_distinguishes_confident_from_flat — sharpness > 0 (fixture varies p_yes ∈ {0.5, 0.58, 0.66, 0.74, 0.82}; the flat-50% case has sharpness=0 — pinned by test_sharpness_zero_for_all_50_percent_forecasts in test_report_calibration.py).

Final-verification close conditions:
- test_dogfood_full_repo_suite_passes runs the full suite as a subprocess and confirms exit code 0.
- All listed hardening beads closed: trade-trace-8dv, 4qf, bwv, 268, r0v, 89f, 64q, jzn, l9q, aa2 (per bd state — every dependency cited in the bead description is in ✓ status).

Material findings during dogfood:
- Discovered that the deterministic-replay fixture caused all 5 binary forecasts to be flagged late_recorded (forecast.created_at == outcome.created_at under frozen CLOCK_OVERRIDE → fc_ts >= out_ts passes). Fix: src/trade_trace/tools/fixture.py advances CLOCK_OVERRIDE to _ANCHOR + (7+i) days for each outcome.add call so the late-recorded comparator correctly distinguishes forecast time from outcome time.
- This was a fixture bug, not an impl bug — the late-recorded check is doing its job. Updated fixture, all 5 forecasts now score cleanly.

Full repo: 833 tests passing in 11.40s on the de-deselected run; the recursive test_dogfood_full_repo_suite_passes brings the total to 834.

title: Epic: Implement Trade Trace MVP learning loop from docs
description:
Narrative/root index for building Trade Trace from the current planning docs into an MVP that proves the agent-native local journal → scoring/reporting → memory/recall → playbook learning loop. This epic is the root index, not the executable starting task. Labels are an index only; task dependencies, QC gates, and final verification are authoritative for execution order. Scope is PRD M0-M4. P1/P2 features (imports implementation, risk/opportunity reports, compare, review.bundle implementation, multi-class/scalar scoring, ForecastBench export, sync, HTTP/SSE, web viewer) are out of scope except import-ready schemas/contract stubs explicitly required by the MVP docs. Close rule: final verification closes first; close this epic only after all related material beads are closed, explicitly deferred, or superseded with notes. Canonical query: bd list --label trade-trace-mvp --status open --flat --limit 0 --sort id. Execution entrypoint: bd ready; expected first executable leaves are resolve-contract-gaps and define-dogfood-protocol, then M0 package/contracts.

## Program rules
- This epic is the narrative/root index, not the executable starting task.
- Task dependencies, QC gates, and final verification are authoritative for execution order.
- Labels are an index only; non-blocking relates_to links provide navigation.
- Add future program beads with labels trade-trace-mvp,mvp-build,program + relates_to epic + dependency wiring to the relevant gate/final verifier.

## Canonical child query
`bd list --label trade-trace-mvp --status open --flat --limit 0 --sort id`

## Execution entrypoint
Use bd ready; expected first executable leaves are resolve-contract-gaps and define-dogfood-protocol.

## Close rule
Close final-mvp-verification first; close epic only after related program beads are closed, explicitly deferred, or superseded with notes.
## Success Criteria
- Final Verification: Trade Trace MVP dogfood scenario and contract audit closes with evidence.
- All related program beads are closed, explicitly deferred, or superseded with notes.
- No open trade-trace-mvp material bead remains without a documented deferral/supersession.

title: [EPIC] Trades page overhaul: position-first human-readable Console
description:
# [EPIC] Trades page overhaul: position-first human-readable Console

## Purpose
Make the Console Trades page human-readable by defaulting to position lifecycle rows while preserving decision-event audit access.

## Role and execution model
This epic is the narrative/root index, not the executable starting task. Task dependencies, QC beads, and gate/final-verification beads are authoritative for sequencing. Labels are an index only.

Canonical child query: `bd list --label trades-page-overhaul --status open --flat --limit 0 --sort id`.

Execution entrypoint: `Use bd ready --label trades-page-overhaul; expected first executable leaf is lock-trades-page-contract.`.

Membership model: labels for indexing + non-blocking relates_to links for navigation; task dependencies/gates are authoritative for execution order.

## Product direction accepted for this program
- Keep `/trades` as the human-facing route, defaulting to position lifecycle rows.
- Preserve the existing decision-event audit surface as `/trades?view=events` and keep `/api/console/trades` as the decision-event endpoint.
- Add `/api/console/positions` for the position-row list and enrich `/api/console/positions/{position_id}` for detail cards.
- Preserve read-only/local boundaries: no broker integration, no live marks, no writes, no public/network action, and no frontend-only financial math.
- Backend read models own lifecycle semantics and caveat/copy metadata; the frontend must not stitch N+1 detail calls or infer positions from raw decision rows.

## Scope
Position list API, enriched position detail API, filter/query serialization, position-first `/trades` table, decision-event escape hatch, human-readable detail card, caveat/hygiene rendering, browser QA, docs truthfulness, product/architecture review, and final verification.

## Non-goals
Broker/API integrations, trade execution, live market data, cloud/social features, saved server-side preferences, journal writes, schema migrations unless a later explicit bead is created, LLM trading advice, or frontend-only financial math.

## Graph strategy
ux_hardening: The feature is primarily a user-visible Console redesign, but contract/API foundation work must land before UI to avoid frontend guesswork and N+1 fetching.

## Human decision readiness
Overall: afk_capable.

Resolved decisions:
- Keep `/trades` as the user-facing route, but do not redefine `/api/console/trades`; it remains the decision-event endpoint. (source: Plan review against docs/architecture/reporting-product.md and current serve.py routes.)
- Default `/trades` UI to positions/round-trip rows backed by new `/api/console/positions`; preserve decision-event escape hatch with `?view=events`. (source: User goal plus implementation-readiness review.)
- No broker integration, no live marks, no write actions, no frontend-only financial math. (source: Console read-only product boundary.)
- Use backend CAVEAT_GLOSSARY severity/copy as source of truth and keep current severities to `info|warning` unless a separate contract expands tests/docs. (source: src/trade_trace/console/reporting/metric_glossary.py.)

Defaulted decisions:
- Array query parameter encoding for table endpoints. -> default: Use repeated query parameters for arrays, e.g. `decision_type=paper_enter&decision_type=add`, and update frontend pageQuery/tableFilterParams to preserve arrays.; rationale: FastAPI handles repeated params cleanly and it avoids lossy first-item truncation.; reversal cost: low
- Demo-data detection. -> default: Use the current fixture-name heuristic only for a banner; do not add an `is_seed` schema field in this program.; rationale: The overhaul is additive/read-only and no migration is needed.; reversal cost: low

Irreducible human decisions: none.
Credential/external access needs: none.

## Close rule
Close final verification first; close this epic only after all related program beads are closed, explicitly deferred, or superseded with notes.

## Success Criteria
- Default `/trades` is understandable as one row per journal-projected position lifecycle, with outcome/status and hygiene visible without raw IDs dominating the scan.
- `/trades?view=events` still exposes trade-typed decision rows for audit/power-user workflows.
- API/filter/caveat semantics are documented and tested; arrays are not silently truncated.
- Detail cards use one enriched backend response and preserve raw event JSON as secondary audit data.
- Tests, browser QA, docs truthfulness, product/architecture review, and final verification close before this epic closes.

## Related context
Conceptually adjacent, but not graph-linked to avoid noisy cross-epic traversal: `trade-trace-umzf` and children cover agent-facing current exposure/open-trades CLI/MCP behavior. This epic is scoped to the human Console Trades page.
notes: Epic closeout: trades-page-overhaul completed locally. Fresh post-final scoped proof: bd ready --json --label trades-page-overhaul --exclude-type epic returned only the root epic as administrative/container noise; bd list --status open --json --label trades-page-overhaul returned only this root epic; bd list --status in_progress --json --label trades-page-overhaul returned []; bd dep cycles = no cycles; git status clean. Final verifier trade-trace-g09k closed after targeted verification: backend/API pytest matrix 65 passed, frontend build passed with existing large chunk warning, NODE_ENV=test frontend tests 3 files/8 tests passed, browser/API smoke passed for /trades default positions mode, position detail expansion via one /api/console/positions/{position_id} call, and /trades?view=events decision-event escape hatch. Related implementation, QC, browser QA, docs truthfulness, and product/architecture review beads are closed with evidence. No deferred or superseded blockers were identified. Caveats: program-scoped local verification only; no full repo suite, full accessibility audit, or production/deployed readiness claim.
close_reason: trades page overhaul completed and final verification passed

title: Add end-to-end tests for answering open-trades questions
description:
## Problem
This feature is successful only if an agent can answer common human questions correctly from the public CLI/MCP surface, not just if individual SQL/report functions work.

## Test scenarios
Create fixtures or integration tests that exercise:
1. Empty journal / no open positions.
2. One open paper position with fresh mark.
3. One open actual-recorded position with explicit record-only caveat.
4. Closed position that must not appear in open positions.
5. Watch-only idea that appears in watchlist but not open exposure.
6. Duplicate entry decisions or entry decisions without projection rows, which should appear as anomalies.
7. Stale/missing mark on an open position.

## Acceptance criteria
- Tests call the CLI and/or MCP tool surface agents will use, not only private Python functions.
- Expected output includes stable counts, bucket names, IDs, and caveat codes.
- A golden/no-regression case verifies the exact user-facing distinction: "canonical open positions: zero" vs "recent journal entries exist but are not open positions".
- Tests cover both JSON envelope validity and enough content for an LLM summary.

title: Surface decision/projection ambiguity as first-class anomalies
description:
## Problem
The trader journal currently has dogfood entries where `paper_enter` / `actual_enter` decisions exist but `positions` and `position_events` are empty. Some `actual_enter` rows are record-only and explicitly not external executions. That is exactly the sort of state an agent should not silently interpret.

## Desired behavior
Current-exposure tooling should surface ambiguity rather than force the LLM to infer:
- entry decisions with no linked position event
- duplicate entry decisions on the same instrument/run/price
- `actual_*` decisions with metadata/reason indicating record-only or not externally executed
- open positions without marks
- positions whose latest snapshot/mark is stale
- projection tables empty or stale relative to decision/event history

## Acceptance criteria
- A read-only anomaly section/tool returns stable codes plus affected IDs.
- The output distinguishes product/data-quality caveats from actual market/trade risk.
- The current local dogfood pattern would produce a clear caveat like `entry_decisions_without_positions` rather than tempting the agent to call them open trades.
- Tests cover duplicate entry, record-only actual, missing position_event, missing mark, and clean journal states.

title: Add a current-exposure report for trader-agent questions
description:
## Problem
A user rarely asks in database terms. They ask: "what have you been trading recently?" and "what are my open trades?" A single open-position list is necessary, but not sufficient: the agent also needs recent activity, watch ideas, and ambiguity warnings in one packet.

## Desired interface
Add a read-only report such as:

```bash
tt report current_exposure --actor-id agent:trader
```

Optional args could include `recent_limit`, `include_watchlist`, `include_anomalies`, and filters from `report.filter_schema`.

## Desired output shape
- `summary.open_position_count`
- `summary.watch_count`
- `summary.recent_trade_decision_count`
- `summary.anomaly_count`
- `open_positions[]`
- `watchlist[]`
- `recent_trade_activity[]`
- `anomalies[]`
- `agent_answer_hints[]` or compact human-safe summary fields

## Acceptance criteria
- The report can answer both "open trades" and "recently traded" without a second raw query.
- The report clearly says when recent entries are journal activity but not canonical open exposure.
- The report includes enough IDs to drill into position, decision, thesis, forecast, and instrument details.
- `report.pnl` and `report.watchlist` can remain lower-level primitives, but the current-exposure report is the recommended trader-agent entry point.

title: Expose row-level open positions via CLI and MCP
description:
## Problem
`tt report pnl` exposes `open_position_count`, but not a convenient row-level list of current open trades. The console has read-model code for position detail, but agents do not have a clear CLI/MCP one-liner.

## Desired interface
Add an agent-facing read-only tool such as one of:

```bash
tt position list --status open --actor-id agent:trader
tt report open_positions --actor-id agent:trader
```

MCP should expose the same capability with schema-discoverable args.

## Output fields
Each open position row should include:
- position_id, instrument_id, market title, symbol, venue
- kind (`paper`, `actual`, `simulation`) and side
- net quantity / size, avg_entry_price, opened_at, updated_at
- latest mark source/timestamp if known, unrealized_pnl, realized_pnl
- opening decision_id, thesis_id, forecast_id where available
- caveats: missing mark, missing risk, stale mark, record-only actual, projection stale, etc.

## Acceptance criteria
- No raw SQLite query is needed to list open positions.
- The no-open-positions case returns a positive empty result with `count: 0`, not an error.
- Output includes examples/rows sufficient for an agent to summarize without another query.
- Tests cover no positions, open paper position, open actual-recorded position, closed position excluded by default, and stale/missing mark caveats.

title: Define current-exposure semantics for agents
description:
## Problem
"Open trades" is overloaded in the journal today. The system has decisions, forecasts, theses, snapshots, positions, watchlist reports, and record-only actual entries. Agents need a documented contract that says which table/tool wins for each user intent.

## Proposed semantics
Define a contract for at least these buckets:

- `open_positions`: canonical `positions.status = open`; this is current exposure.
- `closed_positions`: canonical `positions.status = closed`; historical exposure.
- `watchlist`: `decision.type = watch`; not exposure.
- `recent_trade_activity`: recent `paper_enter`, `paper_exit`, `actual_enter`, `actual_exit`, `add`, `reduce`, `hold`, etc.; not necessarily current exposure.
- `record_only_actual`: actual-entry/exit rows that are journaled but not externally executed by Trade Trace; should carry explicit metadata/caveat when available.
- `projection_anomalies`: decision/event/projection mismatches needing human/product attention.

## Acceptance criteria
- A doc or schema description states precedence: positions projection is canonical for current exposure; decisions are activity/audit trail; watch decisions are not positions.
- The report/tool output uses stable machine-readable bucket names and caveat codes.
- The contract covers the no-position case, open paper positions, open actual-recorded positions, watch-only ideas, duplicate decisions, and projection missing/stale states.
- The language is clear enough to include in MCP `tool.schema` descriptions and CLI help/human hints.

title: Make current trading exposure agent-native
description:
## Product intent
As the trader agent, I want one reliable current-state surface for questions like:

- "what have you been trading recently?"
- "what open trades do you have?"
- "what am I exposed to right now?"
- "what is just a watch idea versus an actual/paper position?"

The current product makes the agent stitch together `report.pnl`, `report.watchlist`, raw decision rows, and the `positions` projection. That invites exactly the wrong failure mode: the agent starts interpreting unclosed entry decisions heuristically instead of giving a crisp, auditable answer.

## Agent-first desired behavior
A single agent-facing read path should return a current-exposure packet with explicit buckets:

1. **Open positions**: canonical rows from the positions projection, with kind (`paper`, `actual`, `simulation`), side, size/net quantity, average entry, marks/P&L, latest snapshot age, and caveats.
2. **Watchlist / no-position ideas**: watch decisions that are intentionally not exposure.
3. **Recently recorded trade decisions**: recent entries/exits as journal activity, clearly separate from current exposure.
4. **Anomalies / ambiguity**: entry decisions not represented in positions, duplicate entries, record-only actuals, stale/missing marks, projection rebuild warnings.
5. **Suggested agent wording**: enough summary text or stable fields that an LLM can answer candidly without inventing semantics.

## Success criteria
- A trader agent can answer "open trades" without raw SQLite or decision-log inference.
- The tool makes "canonical open position" versus "watch idea" versus "record-only/ambiguous journal artifact" unmistakable.
- The current no-open-positions case is boring and precise, not a heuristic explanation.
- Existing `report.pnl` remains useful but is no longer the only route to exposure detail.

title: Add one-line open trades / open positions report
description:
## Problem
A trader asked for "open trades" and the agent had to choose between multiple interpretations:

- canonical open positions from the `positions` projection
- watchlist decisions (`report.watchlist`)
- entry decisions with no corresponding exit decision
- record-only `actual_enter` dogfood rows that are explicitly not externally executed

There is a one-line `tt report pnl` command that reports `summary.metrics.open_position_count`, but it does not provide a concise row-level open-trades list with market title, side, size, entry price, opened_at, status, mark coverage/caveats, and linked decision/thesis IDs. `tt report watchlist` is a different concept and returns only watch decisions.

## Evidence from local trader journal
- `tt report pnl --actor-id agent:trader` returns `open_position_count: 0` and no groups.
- `tt report watchlist --actor-id agent:trader` returns no watch rows.
- Tool registry has `report.pnl` and `report.watchlist`, but no obvious `position list`, `position open`, `report.positions`, or `report.open_trades` CLI/MCP tool.
- The code has a read-only console position detail model in `src/trade_trace/console/reporting/position_rows.py`, but it is not exposed as an agent-facing CLI/MCP one-liner.
- The dogfood journal contains `paper_enter` / record-only `actual_enter` decisions, so agents can be tempted to infer "unclosed entries" from decisions when they should prefer a canonical projection/report.

## Recommendation
Add an agent-facing read-only report/tool, e.g. `report.open_positions` or `position.list`, with CLI invocation such as:

```bash
tt position list --status open --actor-id agent:trader
# or
tt report open_trades --actor-id agent:trader
```

Expected output should include both machine-readable fields and compact examples:
- position_id, instrument_id, market title/symbol, venue
- kind (`paper` / `actual` / simulation), side, quantity/net size, avg_entry_price
- opened_at, updated_at, status, closed_at if any
- latest mark / unrealized_pnl / open mark coverage caveat
- opening decision_id, thesis_id, forecast_id if available
- caveats for missing mark, missing risk, record-only semantics, or projection staleness

## Acceptance criteria
- A single documented CLI/MCP tool lists current open positions without requiring raw SQLite queries.
- Agents can answer "what open trades do I have?" from the tool output without heuristic decision-log inference.
- `report.pnl` either links to the new detailed tool when open_position_count > 0 or includes row examples sufficient for a human-readable answer.
- Tests cover journals with: no positions, open paper position, closed position, duplicate entry decision, watch-only decision, and record-only actual entry.
notes: Folded into trader-agent current-exposure brainstorm epic trade-trace-umzf; keep this bead as the concrete MVP row-level open-positions command slice.

title: Design contract: venue-agnostic market-scan dry-run/promote journal bundle
description:
Define the contract for a guided, venue-agnostic market-scan flow that turns caller-supplied market snapshot and research/source inputs into a complete auditable journal arc without fetching market data, giving trading advice, or executing trades.

The design must choose and document the public tool surface, recommended as a read-only `market.scan.dry_run` plus write-capable `market.scan.promote`, and split implementation into narrow beads. It must specify dry-run/promote semantics, deterministic child idempotency keys, CLI/MCP schema shape, no-fetch/no-advice/no-execution boundaries, and how `watch`, `skip`, and `paper_enter` materialize through existing primitive rows.

Polymarket is allowed only as a caller-supplied mapping example; Trade Trace must not fetch Polymarket data in this program.
acceptance_criteria: Contract names and payloads are documented; covers `watch`, `skip`, and `paper_enter`; Polymarket mapping is documented as caller-supplied data only; dry-run output includes primitive call plan, checks/warnings, child idempotency keys, and promote payload hint; promote output includes created/reused IDs and final bundle status; implementation beads are narrow and sequenced.
notes:
Implemented design-contract artifact for trade-trace-ebc1.1.

Added docs/architecture/market-scan-contract.md specifying:
- public surfaces market.scan.dry_run and market.scan.promote
- no-fetch/no-advice/no-execution/no-credentials boundaries
- normalized input bundle model for venue/instrument/snapshot/sources/thesis/forecast/decision
- dry-run read-only semantics with ordered primitive call plan, checks/warnings, missing fields, deterministic child idempotency keys, promote_payload_hint, and promote_hash
- promote transaction/idempotency/rollback semantics with created/reused IDs, primitive results, and raw journal.bundle.status final check
- watch/skip/paper_enter materialization through existing decision.add semantics, including paper_enter's existing position event/projection behavior
- child idempotency key scheme and Polymarket caller-supplied mapping example with explicit no fetcher
- downstream sequencing for .2-.7 aligned to current Beads.

Linked the new doc from README.md and docs/PRD.md.

Verification:
- independent read-only review initially found two blockers; both were fixed and targeted re-review returned PASS
- required-term/readback check: pass, missing=[]
- watch example forbidden fields check: pass
- README/PRD link check: pass
- git diff --check: pass
- no Markdown linter configured/found; no runtime code changed, so full tests were not run for this docs-only bead
close_reason: Design contract artifact added and independently reviewed.

title: Bundle status may still describe a completed watch arc as partial/missing
description:
## Summary
Bundle status may still describe a completed watch arc as partial/missing

