title: Refactor memory.recall into behavior-preserving parse/rank/shape/write helpers
description:
Context:
Follow-up discovered by trade-trace-y0b2 memory.recall decomposition investigation. Artifact: docs/audits/simplification-20260520T181054Z/memory-recall-decomposition-investigation.md.

Current issue:
_memory_recall mixes parse/options, provider/strategy selection, ranking orchestration, budget/provenance shaping, recall-event/stats writes, response construction, and meta_hints. The investigation found it is safe to split only after pinning ordering semantics that existing tests do not fully characterize.

Scope:
- Add characterization tests for current ordering interactions before refactoring.
- Extract behavior-preserving helpers around parse/rank/shape/write/response phases.
- Keep existing ranking helpers/constants unchanged.
- Preserve DB transaction scope and no-network/semantic-provider behavior.

Non-goals:
- Do not change ranking algorithms, constants, semantic provider behavior, or no-network defaults.
- Do not change recall event/stats semantics or output envelope shape.

Required characterization tests:
1. min_confidence is applied after top-k selection.
2. compact is applied before max_chars.
3. max_chars stops at first overflow and does not skip to later smaller items.
4. max_chars applies even with include_body=False.
5. per_strategy lists remain capped raw strategy rankings and are not filtered by confidence/budget.
6. recall events/stats count only emitted item ids after confidence/budget filters.

Suggested helper split:
- _parse_recall_options(args) -> RecallOptions
- _build_recall_rankings(conn, options, in_scope_rows, provider) -> rankings
- _score_ranked_nodes(conn, rankings, in_scope_rows) -> scored
- _shape_recall_items(conn, scored_top, in_scope_rows, options) -> (items, chars_used)
- _write_recall_event_and_stats(conn, ..., items, rankings, options, ctx, seg) -> (recall_id, created_at)
- _build_recall_response(...); _set_recall_meta_hints(...)

Validation:
- ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/integration/test_memory_recall_budgets.py tests/integration/test_memory_retrieval_constants.py tests/integration/test_memory_layer.py tests/integration/test_projection_rebuild.py tests/integration/test_reproducibility_replay.py -q
- If strategy/provider selection is touched: ./.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/security/test_embeddings_off_by_default.py tests/security/test_no_network_default.py tests/integration/test_embeddings_sqlite_vec_substrate.py tests/security/test_embeddings_api_keyring.py -q

Provenance:
Discovered from trade-trace-y0b2; belongs to repo-simplification 2026-05-20 backlog under trade-trace-w3vs and should block final simplification verification trade-trace-z5bd until resolved.
acceptance_criteria: Characterization tests pin the documented ordering semantics, helper split lands without behavior/output/provider/transaction changes, and listed validation commands pass.
notes: Implemented behavior-preserving memory.recall helper split in src/trade_trace/tools/memory.py with RecallOptions plus parse/rank/score/shape/write/response/meta helpers. Added characterization tests for min_confidence after top-k, compact before max_chars, first-overflow max_chars break, max_chars with include_body=false, raw per_strategy lists, and emitted-only recall event/stats. Controller validation: memory recall/retrieval/layer/projection/reproducibility matrix => 97 passed; embeddings/no-network provider matrix => 22 passed; ruff changed files => passed. Independent read-only review found no behavior blockers; import-order blocker fixed.
close_reason: fixed

title: Document beta dogfood artifact retention convention
description:
## Context
The inventory artifact for `trade-trace-73zr` found no obvious repo-local beta dogfood transcripts/artifacts under filenames containing dogfood, beta, or transcript. The program is explicitly beta-feedback-driven, so future contract-drift and smoothness-eval work needs a durable, documented place to cite beta dogfood evidence.

## Scope
- Define a repo-safe artifact retention convention for beta/dogfood evidence used by the agent-native workbench ergonomics program.
- Update the relevant docs/eval instructions so future inventory/eval beads know where to look and what not to commit publicly.
- Preserve privacy: do not commit raw private transcripts, secrets, account identifiers, or local absolute paths. Use sanitized summaries or ignored local run directories where appropriate.

## Out of scope
- Creating fake beta artifacts.
- Publishing private transcripts.
- Implementing unrelated schema/help/tool changes.
- Building a full telemetry or analytics pipeline.

## Acceptance criteria
- A documented beta/dogfood artifact retention path or convention exists for this repo/program.
- The convention states what evidence can be checked in versus kept ignored/local, and how future Beads notes should cite sanitized evidence.
- `trade-trace-73zr`'s evidence gap is addressed by a future-facing process rather than hidden in prose.
- Relevant docs/eval instructions are updated without exposing private data.

## Validation
- Inspect docs/eval guidance and the final artifact path/convention.
- Run any touched docs/link tests if markdown links are added or changed.

## Provenance
Discovered by `trade-trace-73zr` inventory artifact `docs/audits/agent-workbench-contract-drift-20260520T1846Z.md`, section 6/7.
acceptance_criteria: Documented beta/dogfood artifact retention convention exists; it distinguishes checked-in sanitized evidence from ignored/local private evidence; future Beads notes know how to cite sanitized evidence; relevant docs/eval guidance updated without exposing private data.
notes: Created tracked docs/architecture/agent-workbench-dogfood-evidence.md and added docs/AGENT_GUIDE.md pointer. Controller validation: docs link test passed (3 tests); targeted content check confirms privacy boundary, checked-in vs ignored/private evidence rules, raw-run naming, sanitized summary template, Beads citation convention, eval usage, and trade-trace-73zr gap linkage.
close_reason: dogfood evidence convention documented

title: Reconcile residual report and test-helper simplification findings against 2026-05-19 backlog
description:
Context:
Cross-domain reconciliation. Candidates SIMP20-014, SIMP20-015, SIMP20-016, SIMP20-027, and SIMP20-028. Created because the 2026-05-20 review found residual simplification signals that overlap the closed 2026-05-19 simplification backlog.

Current complexity / evidence:
- Closed `trade-trace-qnxt` says report-row/result helper extraction completed, but lane_3 still found report adapter boilerplate and report envelope repetition in tools/reports.py and report modules.
- Closed `trade-trace-x0po` says report filter support declarations were simplified, but lane_3 still found review.bundle filter error conversion drift from report tools.
- Closed `trade-trace-qs5v` says shared initialized_home fixture landed and 20 files migrated, but current AST still finds 37 `home`, 20 `_mcp`, 11 `_envelope`, and 8 `_db` helpers in tests.

Why reconciliation-first:
These may be legitimate residual next-step simplifications, intentionally retained explicit contract examples, or incomplete acceptance from prior closed beads. Creating many duplicate beads would pollute the graph without deciding whether to reopen, supersede, or accept the residual state.

Target investigation:
Compare current code against closed beads `qs5v`, `qnxt`, and `x0po`. Decide for each residual cluster whether:
1. existing closed work is complete and residual duplication is intentional;
2. reopen/update an existing bead is appropriate;
3. create one or more new narrow follow-ups with exact removal lists and validation; or
4. reject/defer.

Non-goals:
- Do not implement report/test helper refactors in this bead.
- Do not reopen closed beads without explicit decision notes and evidence.
- Do not remove contract-example duplication blindly.

Validation / findings required:
- For tests: classify exact duplicate helper bodies vs intentional local examples; preserve per-test isolation.
- For reports: compare residual adapter/envelope/filter patterns against qnxt/x0po acceptance and implementation scope.

Acceptance criteria:

- Any downstream bead has an exact file/helper cluster list and validation commands.
- No production/test refactor is performed under this reconciliation bead.
- Closed 2026-05-19 beads are not contradicted silently; any incompleteness is documented.

title: Investigate shared forecast add/supersede write kernel without breaking atomicity
description:
Context:
Domain: ledger-tools-projections. Candidate SIMP20-009. Investigation/design-first.

Current complexity:
forecast.add and forecast.supersede duplicate forecast row/outcome/payload/auto-score mechanics, but supersede is intentionally all-in-one to avoid a known two-transaction lineage corruption window.

Evidence:
- src/trade_trace/tools/ledger.py:568-726 _forecast_add.
- src/trade_trace/tools/ledger.py:1567-1805 _forecast_supersede.
- src/trade_trace/tools/ledger.py:1581-1587 comments say auto-scoring is intentionally not replicated, but current implementation at 1755-1791 does replicate late auto-score path.
- src/trade_trace/tools/ledger.py:602-619 builds forecast.add payload while 1701-1717 rebuilds equivalent payload inline.

Why investigation-first:
This touches idempotency, event ordering, scoring side effects, and UnitOfWork atomicity. A direct refactor without characterization could reintroduce the old two-transaction bug.

Target investigation:
Characterize exact current behavior for forecast.add and forecast.supersede, especially idempotency replay, supersedes edge/event ordering, resolved_final auto-score behavior, payload shape, and single-transaction guarantees. Decide whether a shared internal write kernel is safe and create downstream implementation beads only after characterization.

Non-goals:
- Do not implement the refactor in this investigation.
- Do not change forecast semantics, event ordering, or scoring.

Validation / findings required:
- tests/contracts/test_event_enum_coverage.py::test_forecast_superseded_event_emitted
- tests/integration/test_ledger_event_emission.py
- Add/confirm a regression for supersede against an already resolved_final outcome verifying auto_scored and forecast.scored behavior.
- Findings must document whether `_insert_forecast_core` or equivalent helper is safe, with exact behavior-preservation criteria.

Acceptance criteria:
- Findings record current forecast.add/supersede behavior and risks.
- Decision says implement shared kernel, defer, or reject, with evidence.
- Any downstream implementation bead includes exact validation commands and ordering/idempotency constraints.
- No production code behavior changes under this investigation.

Provenance:
Discovered by repo-simplification-review candidate SIMP20-009 in lane ledger-tools-projections.
acceptance_criteria: See body acceptance criteria.
notes: Investigation artifact committed at docs/audits/simplification-20260520T181054Z/forecast-add-supersede-write-kernel-investigation.md. title: Split journal.fixture_seed into composable deterministic builder profiles
description:
Context:
Domain: tests-docs-build. Candidate SIMP20-029.

Current complexity:
The deterministic fixture seed substrate is monolithic and supports dogfood, diagnostics, console reporting, and browser tests in one large module.

Evidence:
- src/trade_trace/tools/fixture.py is 687 lines.
- It mixes deterministic clock/id generation, generic dispatch wrapper, mvp-eval base seed, mvp-eval-rich overlay, diagnostic source/outcome fixtures, and reporting/console position fixtures.
- tests/integration/test_fixture_seed.py is 282 lines.
- Fixture seed is consumed by tests/console_browser/conftest.py, console reporting adapter/read-model tests, and console HTTP route tests.
- Targeted lane validation: python3 -m pytest tests/integration/test_fixture_seed.py -q => 9 passed, 1 skipped.

Why simplification is safe/desirable:
A single broad seed makes narrow tests depend on unrelated fixture details. Splitting internals into deterministic builder profiles reduces coupling while preserving the public tool contract.

Target simplification:
Keep public journal.fixture_seed behavior stable, but split internals into deterministic IDs/clock helpers, base journal primitives, diagnostic overlays, reporting/position overlay, and a profile registry mapping target -> ordered builders.

Non-goals:
- Do not reduce public mvp-eval or mvp-eval-rich coverage without a product decision.
- Do not change deterministic IDs/counts/hashes unless explicitly approved and tested.
- Do not change Console product behavior.

Behavior preservation:
- Existing fixture targets produce the same rows or explicitly documented equivalent deterministic output.
- Tests using mvp-eval-rich continue to pass.

Validation:
- python3 -m pytest tests/integration/test_fixture_seed.py -q
- python3 -m pytest tests/integration/test_console_reporting_adapter.py tests/integration/test_console_reporting_read_model.py tests/contracts/test_console_http_routes.py -q
- Verify deterministic hashes/counts remain identical for mvp-eval and mvp-eval-rich, or document approved intentional deltas.

Acceptance criteria:
- fixture.py internals are split into composable deterministic builders/profiles.
- Public journal.fixture_seed targets and outputs are preserved or intentionally characterized.

title: Single-source Console route catalog and static asset provenance guard
description:
Context:
Domain: console-backend-frontend. Candidates SIMP20-022 and SIMP20-023.

Current complexity:
The Console route/page catalog and packaged static asset source-of-truth are duplicated across backend, frontend, docs, and package data.

Evidence:
- src/trade_trace/console/serve.py:209-220 hard-codes backend app route catalog.
- frontend/console/src/main.tsx:75-89 hard-codes frontend navigation.
- frontend/console/src/main.tsx:380-427 hard-codes frontend route tree.
- docs/CONSOLE.md lists page/API expectations separately.
- src/trade_trace/console/static/app/index.html and assets/console.js are committed separately from frontend source.
- tests/contracts/test_console_shell.py asserts static asset presence but not freshness/provenance.

Why simplification is safe/desirable:
Route catalog drift can break local navigation or fallback behavior, and static-source drift can ship an older Console while source tests pass. Both are source-of-truth simplifications for the same optional local Console surface.

Target simplification:
Introduce a typed frontend route/report definition source that derives nav and route creation. Add a lightweight static asset provenance or release-gate check so packaged static assets are known to correspond to the current frontend build/source contract.

Non-goals:
- Do not add new Console pages or product features.
- Do not change read-only/no-network/no-advice safety posture.
- Do not remove dependencies unless separately covered by deadcode/product-overhaul work.

Behavior preservation:
- Visible routes and report tool mappings remain the same.
- Packaged app serving behavior remains the same.
- Static asset guard should detect drift without changing runtime behavior.

Validation:
- python3 -m pytest tests/contracts/test_console_shell.py tests/contracts/test_console_http_routes.py tests/contracts/test_console_endpoints.py -q
- cd frontend/console && npm test && npm run build
- Verify packaged static asset provenance or rebuild-dirty check is documented and reproducible.

Acceptance criteria:
- Frontend Console nav and route tree are derived from one typed source where practical.
- Backend route fallback/catalog remains aligned with visible app routes or is explicitly reduced to backend-only metadata.
- Static packaged asset freshness/provenance is checked by test, build, or release gate.
- Listed tests/builds pass.
- No Console feature expansion or safety posture change.

Provenance:
Discovered by repo-simplification-review candidates SIMP20-022 and SIMP20-023 in lane console-backend-frontend.
acceptance_criteria: See body acceptance criteria.
notes: Implemented in commit 6973cc9. Added frontend routeCatalog.json/routeCatalog.ts and backend packaged route_catalog.json; frontend nav and route tree derive from the typed catalog, and /api/console/catalog reads packaged metadata. Added Vite provenance generation for source/static asset hashes, package_data inclusion, docs, and shell contract tests comparing catalogs, source hashes, asset hashes, and actual packaged asset set to detect stale extras. Rebuilt packaged console assets. Controller validation: npm frontend tests => 8 passed; npm build passed with existing chunk-size warning; console contract/http/endpoints => 40 passed; independent review hardening gaps fixed and revalidated.
close_reason: fixed

title: Use named row access for positions projection replay
description:
Context:
Domain: ledger-tools-projections. Candidate SIMP20-011. Includes folded cleanup from SIMP20-013 for stale memory_node_stats projection test wording if touched nearby.

Current complexity:
positions projection replay selects a wide tuple and then relies on numeric indexes across invariant-heavy replay code.

Evidence:
- src/trade_trace/projections.py:91-108 selects 13 columns.
- src/trade_trace/projections.py:168 uses row[12] for decision_id.
- src/trade_trace/projections.py:199-200 uses events[0][1] and events[0][10].
- src/trade_trace/projections.py:253 and 265 use row[11] for event IDs in error details.
- lane evidence also found tests/integration/test_projection_rebuild.py still describes memory_node_stats rebuild as deferred/no-op even though src/trade_trace/projections.py:335-393 implements it.

Why simplification is safe/desirable:
Numeric tuple indexes obscure replay invariants and make future SELECT-column changes risky. Named row access or a small typed event record preserves behavior while making the invariant code auditable.

Target simplification:
Use sqlite3.Row, a NamedTuple/dataclass, or constants for projection row fields. Keep SQL ordering and replay algorithm unchanged. Update stale projection test naming/comments only if part of the same touched surface.

Non-goals:
- Do not change position replay semantics.
- Do not change output columns or PnL calculations.
- Do not alter memory_node_stats behavior except stale wording/tests.

Behavior preservation:
- Preserve ORDER BY position_id, created_at, id.
- Preserve invariant errors for same-sign exits and over-close reversals.
- Preserve realized/unrealized PnL None-vs-0 behavior.

Validation:
- python3 -m pytest tests/integration/test_projection_rebuild.py -q
- python3 -m pytest tests/integration/test_memory_layer.py::test_memory_node_stats_rebuildable_from_events -q
- Run representative P&L/watchlist report tests consuming positions projection.

Acceptance criteria:
- Projection replay no longer depends on unexplained numeric indexes for the wide event tuple.
- Behavior and output shape are unchanged.
- Stale memory_node_stats no-op wording is removed if encountered in the same change.
- Listed tests pass.

Provenance:
Discovered by repo-simplification-review candidates SIMP20-011 and folded SIMP20-013 in lane ledger-tools-projections.
acceptance_criteria: See body acceptance criteria.
notes: Implemented PositionEventRow NamedTuple in src/trade_trace/projections.py and replaced numeric wide-tuple replay indexes with named attributes while preserving SELECT order, ORDER BY position_id/created_at/id, invariant errors, and PnL semantics. Controller validation: combined pytest matrix including projection_rebuild, memory_node_stats rebuild, report_pnl_watchlist, and console position detail tests => 119 passed.
close_reason: fixed

title: Extract generic safe path helpers for restore and import containment
description:
Context:
Domain: storage-events-security. Candidate SIMP20-005.

Current complexity:
journal.restore reuses model-import-specific path validation helpers and then wraps/remaps model-oriented errors into restore-specific validation details.

Evidence:
- src/trade_trace/tools/admin.py:129-145 defines _safe_model_relpath and _resolve_under.
- src/trade_trace/tools/admin.py:447-500 journal.restore reuses _safe_model_relpath and manually repeats source-root and home-root containment checks.
- src/trade_trace/tools/admin.py:459-473 catches ToolError from _safe_model_relpath and remaps details for restore.

Why simplification is safe/desirable:
The behavior is good but the ownership is wrong: a model-specific helper now represents a generic manifest/copy-tree path security contract. Generic helpers would reduce cognitive load and reduce future path-validation drift.

Target simplification:
Extract generic safe_relative_path and resolve_under_root helpers. Let model import and journal.restore call the same generic helper while keeping domain-specific error messages/details at call boundaries.

Non-goals:
- Do not relax traversal, absolute path, Windows-drive, symlink, or root-containment checks.
- Do not change ErrorCode.VALIDATION_ERROR behavior for restore manifest path failures.
- Do not redesign restore/import formats.

Behavior preservation:
- Reject empty/non-string, absolute, Windows-drive, and parent-traversal paths.
- Reject resolved source paths outside backup src and destinations outside TRADE_TRACE_HOME.
- Preserve current restore/model error fields or explicitly characterize any difference before implementation.

Validation:
- uv run pytest tests/security/test_restore_manifest_paths.py tests/integration/test_admin_tools.py tests/integration/test_operability_drill.py -q
- Add/confirm direct characterization tests for current restore path error details before refactoring.

Acceptance criteria:
- Generic safe path helpers exist and replace model-specific helper reuse where behavior matches.
- Restore and model import preserve current rejection behavior and error codes/details.
- Listed tests pass.
- No unrelated restore/import behavior changes.

Provenance:
Discovered by repo-simplification-review candidate SIMP20-005 in lane storage-events-security.
acceptance_criteria: See body acceptance criteria.
notes: Implemented generic safe_relative_path and resolve_under_root helpers in src/trade_trace/tools/admin.py; model wrappers preserve INVARIANT_VIOLATION messages/details; journal.restore translates helper failures into restore-specific VALIDATION_ERROR details. Added restore path tests for Windows-drive, empty, and non-string manifest paths. Controller validation: restore/admin/operability plus related combined matrix => 119 passed. Independent read-only security review found no blocking issues; symlink/root escape and Windows/traversal semantics preserved.
close_reason: fixed

title: Centralize POSIX permission helpers across storage, exporter, and admin
description:
Context:
Domain: storage-events-security. Candidate SIMP20-004.

Current complexity:
File and directory permission tightening is implemented in multiple places with slightly different local names and comments.

Evidence:
- src/trade_trace/storage/database.py:51-75 defines _set_user_only_permissions and _set_user_only_dir_permissions.
- src/trade_trace/storage/database.py:78-89 defines _chmod_wal_shm_siblings.
- src/trade_trace/tools/admin.py:49-70 defines _tighten_file and _tighten_dir.
- src/trade_trace/exporter.py:156-208 performs inline chmod behavior for exported files and parent directories.

Why simplification is safe/desirable:
These paths enforce the same local privacy/security boundary: files should be user-only and directories should be user-only where the platform supports chmod. Divergence here creates maintenance and test drag at a security-sensitive boundary.

Target simplification:
Extract a small internal helper module for chmod_user_only_file, chmod_user_only_dir, and any needed parent-dir helper. Replace local copies while preserving best-effort POSIX behavior and error suppression semantics.

Non-goals:
- Do not weaken permissions.
- Do not change backup/export/database filenames or locations.
- Do not change Windows/non-POSIX behavior beyond preserving existing no-op/best-effort semantics.

Behavior preservation:
- Preserve 0600 file and 0700 directory intent.
- Preserve current suppression of OSError/PermissionError/NotImplementedError where applicable.
- Preserve WAL/SHM sibling chmod behavior.

Validation:
- uv run pytest tests/security/test_file_permissions.py tests/integration/test_outbox_export.py tests/integration/test_admin_tools.py -q
- uv run pytest tests/security -q

Acceptance criteria:
- One internal permission helper module owns file/dir chmod semantics.
- storage/database.py, tools/admin.py, and exporter.py use the shared helper where behavior matches.
- Existing permission/security/export/admin tests pass.
- No unrelated security or storage behavior changes.

Provenance:
Discovered by repo-simplification-review candidate SIMP20-004 in lane storage-events-security.
acceptance_criteria: See body acceptance criteria.
notes: Implemented in commit 9650c7b. Added src/trade_trace/_permissions.py with POSIX-only chmod_user_only_file/dir(s) helpers and replaced duplicated chmod logic in storage/database.py, tools/admin.py, and exporter.py. Preserved 0600 file/0700 dir intent, WAL/SHM sibling tightening, exporter date-bucket parent tightening, backup/model dir behavior, and best-effort failure suppression. Controller validation: permission/export/admin matrix => 50 passed; full security suite after follow-up rzkr => 210 passed; ruff scoped Python files => passed; independent read-only review found no blockers.
close_reason: fixed

title: EPIC: repo simplification review 2026-05-20
description:
Context:
Repo simplification review for /home/hermes/code/trade-trace at commit ffcd97420bf44c846451bd5d39596d39437d6e3e on branch main. Initial git state: clean, main tracks origin/main with no reported ahead/behind. Beads DB: /home/hermes/code/trade-trace/.beads (database /home/hermes/code/trade-trace/.beads/embeddeddolt). Initial Beads state: 387 total, 46 open, 0 in_progress, 27 blocked, 341 closed, 19 ready.

Scope:
Review tracked source, tests, frontend console, docs/contracts, build/release config, and Beads overlap for evidence-backed simplification opportunities. This epic indexes simplification review outputs and accepted behavior-preserving remediation work. It is not an executable implementation task.

Simplification definition:
Accept candidates only when current code imposes avoidable cognitive, maintenance, testability, or regression cost without enough compensating value. Candidate rows must include concrete code evidence, complexity cost, bounded simplification shape, behavior-preservation criteria, validation path or explicit gap, duplicate/overlap disposition, and risk/priority.

Reject list:
Pure style/naming preferences, speculative rewrites, large unbounded redesigns, behavior-changing refactors, and complexity justified by compatibility/security/performance/product/framework evidence are rejected or deferred.

Behavior-preservation rule:
All simplification work must preserve current product behavior unless a separate bug/design bead explicitly authorizes behavior change. Runtime behavior-heavy or under-characterized simplifications start as investigation/design tasks.

Graph/navigation rule:
This epic is the narrative/root index. Labels and non-blocking relations navigate the backlog; task-to-task dependencies express real sequencing only. Canonical labels: repo-simplification, simplification:20260520, simplification, behavior-preserving. Domain labels use domain:<domain>; complexity labels use complexity:<class>.

Canonical queries:
- bd list --label simplification:20260520 --status open --flat --limit 0 --sort id
- bd dep list <this-epic-id>
- bd graph <this-epic-id>

Candidate addition rule:
Future candidates must copy the central matrix evidence and include benefit, behavior preservation, validation/gap, and duplicate check. Coordinator-only Beads writes; read-only lane agents do not mutate Beads.

Close rule:
Keep this epic open while simplification tasks remain open. Close only after materialized simplification items are closed, deferred with reasons, or superseded, and final graph/readback verification has been recorded.

Provenance:
Created by repo-simplification-review run 20260520T181054Z.

title: Implement journal timeline/replay for agent auditability
description:
## Purpose
Turn the Journal from a shallow event list into a way to understand what the agent knew and did over time.

## Scope
- Filtered timeline grouped by request/session/subject/actor where local fields support it.
- Expandable event details with payload preview, subject IDs, request IDs, idempotency keys, actors, timestamps, related decisions/forecasts/outcomes/sources, and raw payload access.
- A local “replay” mode that steps through journal events only; no market replay unless already stored locally.

## Out of scope
- External candle replay, writeback annotations, or event edits.
acceptance_criteria: Journal provides detailed, filterable audit/replay views over local events and raw payloads without retaining Logs/Raw JSON as top-level tabs.
notes: Implemented in commit c0d8af3. Journal Console page is now a local audit timeline/replay: exact local filters for request/session/subject/actor/event type, grouping by request/run then available subject then actor then ungrouped, expandable details with payload preview, subject/request/idempotency/actor/model/env metadata, related local decisions/forecasts/outcomes/sources/subject events, raw payload links, and a journal-events-only replay panel. /api/console/events supports exact read-only filters; added /api/console/events/{id}/related; removed Logs and Raw JSON from top-level route catalogs/nav while retaining contextual raw payload access. Controller validation: frontend tests => 8 passed; frontend build passed with existing large-chunk warning; console shell/http/endpoints => 39 passed; ruff scoped backend/tests => passed; independent QA found one grouping copy mismatch, fixed and revalidated.
close_reason: fixed

title: Implement calibration and evidence/provenance dashboards
description:
## Purpose
Help humans judge agent forecast quality and evidence health.

## Scope
- Calibration reliability/bin views, score definitions, scored/unscored/late forecast caveats, and example forecasts where backend reports support them.
- Evidence/source coverage, stale/missing/contradictory/sensitive-source diagnostics only when existing local evidence reports support them.
- Drill down to forecasts/decisions/sources/raw payloads.

## Out of scope
- New scoring formulas, external source validation, or web fetching.
acceptance_criteria: Calibration and Evidence pages use report-specific visuals/tables, explain low-N/coverage limits, and avoid blank or misleading generic charts.
notes: Implemented in commit 8654984. Calibration and Evidence Console pages now use report-specific dashboards instead of generic charts: calibration reliability bins/table/visual, Brier/log/ECE/sharpness definitions, scored example forecasts, late/low-N/integrity caveats, contextual raw/report detail; Evidence shows source-quality summary and diagnostics from raw local report envelope with explicit journal-level/no-filter copy. Fixed review finding by not sending Console filters to report.source_quality and not rendering a misleading Evidence FilterBar; calibration filters remain. Static assets/provenance rebuilt. Controller validation: frontend tests => 8 passed; frontend build passed with existing large-chunk warning; console shell/http/endpoints => 39 passed; independent QA blocker fixed and revalidated.
close_reason: fixed

title: Implement strategy/playbook performance and rule-adherence review
description:
## Purpose
Adapt competitor playbook/edge ideas into local, read-only process review.

## Scope
- Strategy and playbook cards/tables comparing supported counts, P&L/R, calibration, evidence coverage, caveats, and examples.
- Show rule adherence/followed/violated/unknown states only if local playbook/adherence data exists.
- Use association language, not causality or advice.
- Drill down to supporting trades/decisions/events.

## Out of scope
- Strategy recommendations, automated optimization, or changing playbook rules from the console.
acceptance_criteria: Strategies/Playbooks are no longer thin inventories; they show supported process/performance comparisons with caveats and honest unsupported states.
notes: Implemented in commit af81027. Strategies and Playbooks Console pages now lead with report-backed local review sections instead of thin inventories: strategy performance uses existing report.strategy_performance and calibration-by-strategy report.compare data for P&L/performance association, scored forecast/Brier coverage, evidence counts, caveats, and report drilldown; playbook review uses report.playbook_adherence only when local adherence rows exist, showing followed/violated-or-overridden/considered-or-unknown/not-applicable counts and honest empty state when unsupported. QA found unsupported Mean R/win-rate columns and overbroad P&L/R copy; fixed by removing unsupported columns and using supported P&L/performance language. Controller validation: frontend tests => 8 passed; frontend build passed with existing large-chunk warning; console shell/http/endpoints => 39 passed; independent QA re-reviewed with blocker fixed.
close_reason: fixed

title: Implement Risk/R-multiple and position analytics dashboard
description:
## Purpose
Make risk review understandable without overclaiming beyond local journal data.

## Scope
- Render supported R-multiple, expectancy, win/loss/breakeven, payoff, pending-risk, and missing-risk-budget metrics.
- Add histograms/distributions/group tables only from existing report/read-model data.
- Explain missing risk budgets, low sample sizes, and incomplete outcomes prominently.
- Drill into contributing trades/decisions where IDs exist.

## Out of scope
- Portfolio VaR, leverage/margin modeling, live exposure, or risk advice.
acceptance_criteria: Risk page is report-specific, caveated, drilldown-capable, and contains no unsupported portfolio/live-risk modeling.
notes: Implemented in commit ba4605b. Added dedicated Console Risk/R-multiple dashboard for report.risk using supported local report/read-model data only: R metrics, expectancy, win/loss/breakeven/payoff, coverage, pending and missing-risk budget cards, R distribution chart/table, grouped risk contributors, contributing examples/detail drilldowns, caveats, and raw/evidence details. No backend formulas, live exposure, VaR, leverage/margin, or advice added. QA found Overview used unsupported win_rate key; fixed to win_rate_r before commit. Controller validation: frontend tests => 8 passed; frontend build passed with existing chunk warning; console shell/http/endpoints/charting/log contracts => 51 passed; targeted risk/read-model tests from QA => 20 passed.
close_reason: fixed

title: Implement Overview and P&L/performance dashboards from supported local reports
description:
## Purpose
Replace inventory/count cards and generic metric dumps with useful local performance review.

## Scope
- Overview cards/charts for supported P&L/performance/risk/evidence/calibration summaries.
- P&L dashboard for realized/unrealized/total P&L, open/closed positions, grouped performance, and examples where report envelopes/read models support them.
- Trend/calendar/equity-like visualizations only if the local data supports period buckets; otherwise show a clear unsupported state.
- Every card/chart links to a table or contributing examples when supported.

## Out of scope
- Live quotes, brokerage reconciliation, external market candles, or frontend-only P&L math.
acceptance_criteria: Overview and P&L/performance views render report-specific visuals with units/legends/caveats/drilldowns and do not display unsupported trend/calendar metrics as if real.
notes: Implemented in commit c8695d0. Overview now surfaces supported local P&L, risk, strategy, calibration, and evidence rollups with chart/table sections, contributor/evidence detail, and route/table links; P&L report page now shows realized/unrealized/mark-to-market cards, open/closed counts, grouped performance table, raw/evidence drilldown, and explicit unsupported trend/calendar/equity state when report buckets are absent. Independent QA found P&L/risk filter overclaim; fixed before commit: report.pnl/report.risk are called unfiltered and copy states they are local aggregate reports not scoped by global filters today, while supported reports keep filters. Controller validation: frontend tests => 8 passed; frontend build passed with existing chunk warning; console/chart/log route contracts => 51 passed; contracts/integration/golden => 1061 passed, 6 skipped; security => 213 passed; ruff tests => passed.

title: EPIC: exhaustive deadcode hunt 2026-05-20
description:
Context:
Exhaustive repo-deadcode-hunt refresh for Trade Trace at HEAD 73aee82 on 2026-05-20.

Goal:
Materialize the evidence-backed cleanup/docs-truth backlog discovered in the current tracked-file manifest.

Repo / branch / scope:
- Repo: /home/hermes/code/trade-trace
- Branch: main
- Manifest: docs/audits/deadcode-20260520T172715Z/tracked-manifest.json
- Coverage ledger: docs/audits/deadcode-20260520T172715Z/coverage-ledger.jsonl
- Candidate matrix: docs/audits/deadcode-20260520T172715Z/candidate-matrix.json
- Lane packets: docs/audits/deadcode-20260520T172715Z/lane-packets.md

Dead-code definition:
Evidence-backed unreachable, unreferenced, stale, misleading, obsolete, duplicate, unused, or owner-confirmation cleanup surfaces. Grep absence alone was not accepted; candidates needed reference-search scope, entrypoint/public/dynamic caveats, validation command/gap, duplicate disposition, and advisor review.

Disposition model:
- confirmed: concrete repo-local dead/stale evidence with low false-positive risk.
- cleanup-candidate: repo-local non-use but public/future/dynamic caveats require careful implementation.
- needs-owner-confirmation: public/exported/product-intent question before removal.
- merge: candidate merged into an existing open bead instead of duplicated.
- keep/reject: durable matrix-only disposition; no implementation bead.

Bug vs cleanup rule:
Stale docs/CLI contract failures are bugs only when they cause concrete operator/agent command failure. Ordinary unused code is task/cleanup, not bug.

Membership model:
Relation-based narrative epic. Use `bd dep list <this-epic>` or label query; `bd children <this-epic>` may be empty by design.

Canonical query:
`bd list --status open --flat --limit 0 --sort id | grep 'deadcode:exhaustive-20260520'`

Graph rule:
This epic is the root index. The final verification gate depends on materialized candidate beads. Matrix-only/merged/rejected rows are recorded in artifacts and not blockers.

Rule for future candidates:
Do not add speculative cleanup. Add only with evidence + reference-search scope + public/dynamic caveats + safe-removal validation/gap + duplicate check + relation to this epic.

Final close rule:
Close materialized candidate beads first or explicitly merge/defer them. Then run final readback/body-integrity checks and close the final verification gate before closing this epic.
acceptance_criteria: See description acceptance criteria and candidate provenance.
notes: Deadcode hunt execution closeout complete. Final gate trade-trace-4hr9 is closed; materialized candidates hdlx, 0apb, bh7q, and kq8y are closed. Final summary addendum records commits and validation. This relation-indexed narrative epic can close as an administrative container; separate product-opportunity beads remain outside this deadcode scope.

title: Improve low-sample learning-loop report actionability
description:
## Summary
Improve early-sample learning-loop reports so they provide actionable guidance before there is enough data for calibration statistics.

## Product opportunity
Reports are structurally useful, but early journals naturally have tiny samples. In the beta pass, calibration was not meaningful yet, while source quality, watchlist, coach callouts, reflection, and playbook adherence were useful. Trade Trace can better support new journals by making the “small sample but still useful” path explicit: focus on process quality, evidence completeness, stale watches, and next-review prompts before statistical calibration is available.

## Beta evidence
- Polymarket approval-rating beta pass artifact: `/tmp/trade_trace_beta_1779280900.json`
- `report.source_quality` successfully surfaced the intentionally stale source.
- `report.watchlist` surfaced the watch decision.
- `report.coach` produced useful callouts including stale source and forecast coverage, but the broader learning-loop value still depended on manual interpretation.
- Partner observation: reports will become powerful after many decisions, but the product can still help from day one by emphasizing process-quality coaching.

## Candidate direction
Possible product shapes:
- A first-run / low-sample coach mode that prioritizes source coverage, decision completeness, watch review dates, unresolved forecasts, and reflection/playbook hygiene.
- Report language that distinguishes “not enough calibration data” from “here are process issues you can fix now.”
- A `report.next_actions` or richer `report.coach` section with concrete next calls, e.g. attach source, add review_by, resolve outcome later, reflect after outcome, propose/update playbook.
- Optional “journal readiness score” for a specific instrument/decision bundle, focused on auditability rather than trading performance.

## Success criteria
In a future beta pass with only 1-3 decisions, reports still give concrete improvement steps and make it obvious what the agent should do next, without pretending there is enough data for calibration conclusions.

## Scope notes
Feature request / review. Affects report design, coach output, memory/playbook loop, and agent ergonomics. No claim that reports should provide investment advice or profit signals.
acceptance_criteria: Low-sample reports/coach output provide truthful insufficient-data caveats plus concrete process-quality next actions, verified by targeted report tests or dogfood fixtures with 1-3 decisions.
notes: Implemented in commit 0267c26. report.coach now includes low_sample_context with scored forecast count, min sample floor, statistical caveat, and sample forecast IDs, plus structured process-quality next_actions for low-N/early journals: calibration-data insufficiency, unresolved forecasts, stale watches, source-quality diagnostics, missing forecast/thesis links, missing watch review_by, missing reflection, and missing playbook adherence rows. No DB/product surface, external data, advice, profit signals, or statistical overclaiming added; actions are local journal/process hygiene. Controller validation: report coach/calibration/source/watchlist/playbook matrix => 89 passed; strategy/schema concurrent matrix => 41 passed; ruff changed files => passed; independent QA => no blockers, targeted coach tests 10 passed.
close_reason: fixed

title: Add lightweight capture-now enrich-later flow for market ideas
description:
## Summary
Add a lightweight “capture now, enrich later” flow for quick market thoughts and rough trade ideas.

## Product opportunity
The full Trade Trace journal arc is valuable for serious decisions, but it is too much ceremony for casual market scans or early hypotheses. During beta, the complete Polymarket arc was trustworthy but heavy: venue, instrument, snapshot, sources, thesis, forecast, decision, reports, reflection, playbook. A trader-agent will often notice a market worth revisiting before it has enough evidence for a thesis/forecast/decision.

A first-class draft capture flow would make Trade Trace more useful without weakening the audit standard: quick thoughts could enter the system as incomplete/draft records, then be promoted when evidence is sufficient.

## Beta evidence
- Polymarket approval-rating beta pass artifact: `/tmp/trade_trace_beta_1779280900.json`
- The pass produced a high-quality audit trail, but the workflow was appropriate for a deliberate watch decision, not for every quick market scan.
- Partner observation: I would use Trade Trace today for higher-conviction journaling, but not for every casual market scan unless there is a lighter capture path.

## Candidate direction
Possible product shapes:
- `idea.capture` or `journal.capture_market_thought` tool that records a rough market thought, source URL, optional price snapshot, tags, and status `draft` / `needs_enrichment`.
- A `promote` path that turns a draft into structured venue/instrument/snapshot/thesis/forecast/decision records.
- A report such as `report.incomplete_ideas` that lists drafts needing enrichment before they are trusted as forecasts or decisions.
- Clear safety labels so drafts are not confused with committed forecasts or trade decisions.

## Success criteria
In a future beta pass, an agent can capture 3-5 rough Polymarket ideas in under a minute each, then promote one into a full audit trail without retyping context or losing provenance.

## Scope notes
Feature request / product opportunity. Affects CLI, MCP, journal model, reports, and future console/workbench UX. No source changes were made in this review session.
acceptance_criteria: A quick market-thought capture flow exists or is deliberately deferred with evidence; v1 defaults to wrapper-first capture unless investigation proves a first-class draft lifecycle is required; promotion preserves provenance into full journal rows.
notes: Implemented in commit 322d354. Added wrapper-first idea.capture tool using existing local primitives: source.add + memory.retain(observation) + source.attach_to_memory_node provenance edge, with source_id/memory_node_id/edge_id, no_advice_boundary, and promotion next_actions. No draft table/schema migration, external market fetch, trade execution, broker/wallet integration, or investment advice. Review blockers resolved before close: idea.capture now composes source/memory/edge inside one UnitOfWork so downstream validation failure leaves zero partial rows, and supplies a structural content_hash/idea_capture_hash so changed captured content under the same idempotency_key raises IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT while pure replay returns the same IDs. Public MCP metadata sanitized to avoid forbidden secret/credential fragments without weakening runtime rejection. Controller validation: idea_capture integration 5 passed; MCP stdio boundary 16 passed; contract agent/tool-schema/self-description suite 43 passed; ruff changed files passed; CLI help smoke passed; direct MCP idea.capture spec scan showed forbidden fragments false for access_key/credential/credentials/secret/token/transport_hint/mcp_transport_hints.
close_reason: fixed

title: Add guided journal bundle and next-action affordances
description:
## Summary
Add guided journal bundles / next-action affordances so Trade Trace feels like an agent workbench, not only a set of primitives.

## Product opportunity
Trade Trace’s primitive tools are powerful, but a fresh agent has to know the correct sequence and relationships: snapshot before thesis, source attachments, forecast before decision, report review, reflection, playbook update, recall. In beta, the system was useful because I already knew the intended loop. A more self-guiding surface would reduce misuse and make the product feel agent-native.

## Beta evidence
- Polymarket approval-rating beta pass artifact: `/tmp/trade_trace_beta_1779280900.json`
- The complete arc worked, but required orchestration across many tools and careful ID passing.
- Separate bugs were filed for schema/help gaps, but even with those fixed, the agent still has to infer the product-level next step.
- Partner observation: the core ledger works; the product opportunity is turning primitives into an opinionated research workbench.

## Candidate direction
Possible product shapes:
- `journal.bundle.start` / `journal.bundle.status` / `journal.bundle.suggest_next` for a market-level research bundle.
- `review.bundle` output that tells the agent what is missing: no source attached, no forecast, no decision, no reflection, no playbook adherence, stale source, etc.
- A guided `journal.arc.validate` tool that checks whether a market idea is audit-ready before final report/closeout.
- Console/workbench panels later could mirror this same bundle state.

## Success criteria
In a future beta pass, after creating a partial market journal, an agent can call one tool and get a precise checklist of missing/weak steps plus IDs needed for the next calls. The full arc should require less source spelunking and less manual bookkeeping.

## Scope notes
Feature request / product design review. Affects CLI, MCP, reports, console, docs, and the journal mental model. Complements but does not replace existing low-level tools.
acceptance_criteria: A guided bundle/status/next-action affordance exists or is deliberately deferred with evidence; a partial market journal can return missing steps, relevant IDs, and next calls without source spelunking.
notes: Implemented in commit 5c64edb. Added read-only journal.bundle.status guided affordance: accepts decision/forecast/thesis/instrument/source/memory seed IDs and returns status, relevant_ids, checklist, next_calls with carry_forward_ids, idea.capture provenance, and no_advice_boundary. Uses existing local rows/edges/metadata only; no new lifecycle table, migration, external fetch, execution, or advice. Independent QA blocker fixed before close: read-only DB opener no longer creates missing --home directories; thesis/instrument traversal discovers forecasts; metadata LIKE seed matching escapes wildcards. Controller validation: tests/integration/test_journal_bundle_status.py 6 passed; idea_capture/review_bundle/MCP forbidden-fragment subset 21 passed; CLI name/golden journal status 8 passed; ruff changed files passed.
close_reason: fixed

title: Console table filter forms use URL hash state that routes never receive or preserve through pagination
description:
Context:
Visible Console table filter forms for Trades, Logs, and Decisions, plus Trades pagination preservation.

Observed behavior:
Apply Filters writes only the URL hash and refreshes the old query, so server-side rows remain unfiltered. Decisions renders filter controls that the route does not accept. Trades pagination drops active filters even when manually supplied as query params.

Expected behavior:
Visible filter controls produce server-visible, shareable state and pagination preserves active filters.

Evidence:
- `src/trade_trace/console/templates/trades.html:22-36`, `logs.html:10-23`, and `decisions.html:10-14` render `data-filter-form` controls.
- `src/trade_trace/console/static/js/console.js:145-181` prevents form submission, writes form values to `window.location.hash`, then calls `refreshNow()`.
- `src/trade_trace/console/static/js/console.js:100-108` refreshes `window.location.pathname + window.location.search`; URL fragments are not sent to the server and are explicitly omitted.
- `src/trade_trace/console/serve.py:298-326` consumes Trades filters from query parameters; `serve.py:373-381` consumes Logs level/tail from query parameters; `serve.py:164-171` Decisions consumes only cursor/limit despite the template’s decision_type/instrument filters.
- `src/trade_trace/console/templates/trades.html:95-96` renders next-page URL as `?cursor={{ next_cursor }}&limit={{ limit }}`, dropping active `strategy_id`, `instrument_id`, and `decision_type`.

Failure mode / impact:
Operators cannot reliably filter key Console tables; follow-on Trades pages can mix unrelated rows into a filtered review.

## Steps to Reproduce
Static trace:
1. Open `/trades`, `/logs`, or `/decisions`.
2. Fill a visible filter and click Apply filters.
3. `console.js` prevents normal submit, writes `#field=value`, then htmx requests only path + query.
4. Server receives no hash state; rows remain unchanged. For `/decisions`, matching route parameters do not exist.
5. On `/trades?strategy_id=x&limit=1`, the next-page link omits `strategy_id`, so page 2 becomes unfiltered.

Duplicate check:
Compared against open issues and CAND-003. CAND-003 is the report dashboard/export `f=` base64 filter contract; this bead is the table form/query/pagination surface. RAW-L2-002 Trades pagination was merged here.

Suggested fix direction:
Use query parameters or the documented canonical filter state for table filters; make Decisions filters real or remove/disable them; preserve active params in pagination links.

Validation:
`python3 -m pytest tests/contracts/test_console_shell.py tests/contracts/test_console_logs_page.py tests/contracts/test_console_pages.py tests/integration/test_console_reporting_read_model.py -q`

Provenance:
Discovered by repo-bughunt candidates CAND-005 / lane-console-ui-templates-docs-002 and merged RAW-L2-002 / lane-reporting-data-pagination-filters-002.
acceptance_criteria:
- Applying filters on /trades sends strategy_id/instrument_id/decision_type to the server and updates rendered rows.
- Applying filters on /logs sends level/tail to the server and updates rendered rows.
- Decisions filters either work end-to-end via route/context support or are removed/disabled with explicit explanation.
- Trades pagination preserves active filters.
- Tests cover form submit URL behavior, route parameter consumption, and pagination preservation.
notes:
Duplicate-scan disposition for console-20260520T124742Z: bd find-duplicates --threshold 0.35 reports a mechanical overlap with trade-trace-251r. Keep separate: this bead owns table form/query/hash/pagination behavior for Trades/Logs/Decisions; trade-trace-251r owns reporting dashboard/export f= ReportFilter plumbing and export filter provenance. Different route surfaces and validation tests.
Implemented server-visible GET table filters for Trades/Logs/Decisions, added Decisions route/context/SQL filtering, and preserved filter params in Trades/Decisions pagination. Controller validation: console+CLI focused suite => 79 passed. Independent review: PASS.
close_reason: fixed

title: Reporting dashboards never load the documented vendored Chart.js asset
description:
Context:
Console reporting dashboard chart rendering and vendored Chart.js asset contract. Evidence is static/docs-contract evidence; no live browser probe was run.

Observed behavior:
Chart-capable templates emit canvas/config plus `chart-bootstrap.js` only. They do not load the documented local `static/vendor/chartjs/chart.umd.min.js` asset before bootstrap, so `window.Chart` remains undefined unless some out-of-scope injection exists.

Expected behavior:
Chart-capable pages load the local vendored Chart.js asset before `chart-bootstrap.js`, preserving CSP/no-CDN posture and graceful missing-asset degradation.

Evidence:
- `src/trade_trace/console/templates/dashboard.html:32-35` emits canvas, JSON config, and `/static/js/chart-bootstrap.js` only.
- `src/trade_trace/console/static/js/chart-bootstrap.js:65-67` replaces the canvas with a missing-asset caveat when `window.Chart` is undefined.
- `docs/CONSOLE.md:148-158` and `src/trade_trace/console/static/vendor/chartjs/README.md:3-44` document the vendored `static/vendor/chartjs/chart.umd.min.js` asset and chart rendering after install.
- Coordinator static search found `chart.umd` references only in `chart-bootstrap.js` comments/missing-asset copy and the vendor README, not in templates/base shell.

Failure mode / impact:
Reporting dashboard charts remain unavailable after following the documented vendoring step; users get a false missing-asset caveat while numeric tables still render.

## Steps to Reproduce
Static trace:
1. Place `src/trade_trace/console/static/vendor/chartjs/chart.umd.min.js` as documented.
2. Render any chart-capable dashboard such as `/reports/pnl`.
3. HTML includes only `/static/js/chart-bootstrap.js`; no template includes the vendor UMD script.
4. Bootstrap sees `window.Chart === undefined` and replaces canvases with missing-asset caveats.

Duplicate check:
Compared against open issues and prior chart bootstrap/vendor coverage themes. No open duplicate; existing tests check bootstrap scaffolding but not template asset loading order.

Suggested fix direction:
Load `/static/vendor/chartjs/chart.umd.min.js` before `chart-bootstrap.js` when chart configs are emitted, without adding external network references.

Validation:
`python3 -m pytest tests/contracts/test_console_chart_bootstrap.py tests/contracts/test_console_shell.py tests/security/test_console_security_headers.py -q`

Provenance:
Discovered by repo-bughunt candidate CAND-004 / lane-console-ui-templates-docs-001.
acceptance_criteria:
- Chart-capable dashboard HTML references /static/vendor/chartjs/chart.umd.min.js before chart-bootstrap.js or otherwise guarantees window.Chart is defined from the local asset.
- Missing/404 asset still degrades visibly without a 500.
- No CDN or external runtime network reference is introduced.
- Contract tests cover local asset loading order.
notes: Loaded local /static/vendor/chartjs/chart.umd.min.js before chart-bootstrap.js in chart-capable dashboard template; added contract test for local asset order/no external refs. Controller validation: combined focused console suite => 59 passed. Independent review: PASS.
close_reason: fixed

title: Reporting dashboard and export routes ignore documented f= ReportFilter URL state
description:
Context:
Console reporting dashboards and `/reports/<tool>/export.json` filter URL state.

Observed behavior:
`/reports/*?f=...` and `/reports/<tool>/export.json?f=...` silently use empty filters; invalid `f` is never decoded/rejected.

Expected behavior:
Dashboard/export routes decode `f`, pass the decoded `ReportFilter` to report args, reject malformed/unknown filter state, and preserve the originating filter in export packets.

Evidence:
- `docs/CONSOLE.md:128-137` documents `f=<base64url-json>` canonical `ReportFilter` URL state and says unknown axes are rejected.
- `docs/CONSOLE.md:139-146` says export packets bundle the originating filter.
- `src/trade_trace/console/pages.py:447-595` hardcodes `args={"filter": {}}` for P&L, Risk, Calibration, Performance, Strategy, Decision Intelligence, and Compare dashboards.
- `src/trade_trace/console/serve.py:221-276` dashboard/export route signatures have no `f` parameter; Compare accepts only `base_report` and `group_by`, export accepts only `tool`.
- Coordinator AST route-signature probe:
  ```text
  dashboard_pnl_html ['request'] line 222
  dashboard_risk_html ['request'] line 226
  dashboard_performance_html ['request'] line 230
  dashboard_strategy_html ['request'] line 234
  dashboard_decision_intelligence_html ['request'] line 238
  dashboard_calibration_full_html ['request'] line 242
  dashboard_evidence_html ['request'] line 246
  dashboard_compare_html ['request', 'base_report', 'group_by'] line 250
  report_export_json ['tool'] line 276
  ```

Failure mode / impact:
Users can bookmark/share URLs that appear filtered but render/export unfiltered broader data, materially misrepresenting reporting dashboards and audit packets.

## Steps to Reproduce
Static trace:
1. Encode any valid `ReportFilter` with `trade_trace.console.reporting.filter_state.encode_filter`.
2. Request `/reports/pnl?f=<encoded>` or `/reports/report.pnl/export.json?f=<encoded>`.
3. Route signatures do not accept `f`; page builders call report tools with `{}` filters; export packet gets default args.

Duplicate check:
Compared against open issues and prior closed reporting filter bugs. This is distinct from the old report-tool-level “filter echoed but ignored” issue: current failure is Console route/UI URL-state plumbing.

Suggested fix direction:
Wire `decode_filter()` into report-backed dashboard/export routes and surface `FilterStateError` as a typed 400.

Validation:
`python3 -m pytest tests/integration/test_console_reporting_filter_state.py tests/integration/test_console_reporting_adapter.py tests/contracts/test_console_http_routes.py -q`

Provenance:
Discovered by repo-bughunt candidate CAND-003 / lane-reporting-data-pagination-filters-001.
acceptance_criteria:
- Report-backed dashboard routes consume valid f state and pass decoded filters into report args.
- Malformed or unknown-axis f returns a typed 400 instead of silently broadening to no filter.
- Export routes consume the same f and packet filter matches the decoded filter.
- Regression tests prove filtered and unfiltered report calls differ on a rich fixture.
notes: Implemented and controller-verified: dashboard/export f= ReportFilter URL state now decodes via filter_state.decode_filter in serve.py, passes compact filter dicts into report-backed page builders/export packets, returns typed HTTP 400 for malformed/unknown-axis f, sanitizes raw query details, and handles /evidence truthfully as a journal-global report that rejects valid non-empty filters instead of silently broadening. Changed src/trade_trace/console/serve.py, src/trade_trace/console/pages.py, tests/contracts/test_console_http_routes.py. Validation: git diff --check scoped paths passed; python3 -m pytest tests/integration/test_console_reporting_filter_state.py tests/integration/test_console_reporting_adapter.py tests/contracts/test_console_http_routes.py tests/integration/test_console_dashboards.py -q => 75 passed. Independent read-only review: PASS; only non-blocking notes were potential future defensive deep-copy and optional broader route-param smoke coverage.
close_reason: fixed

title: tt console serve --port=0 prints and opens http://host:0 instead of the assigned port
description:
Context:
`tt console serve` startup URL/banner/browser behavior for documented `--port=0`.

Observed behavior:
Dry-run and runtime assembly advertise/open literal `http://127.0.0.1:0/`; if Uvicorn binds an ephemeral port, the Console code never discovers it for the banner/browser/result.

Expected behavior:
Either `--port=0` is rejected with clear guidance, or startup reports and opens the actual OS-assigned port.

Evidence:
- `docs/CONSOLE.md:50-61` says `--port 0` lets the OS pick a free port and recommends it after port conflicts.
- `src/trade_trace/console/serve.py:508-535` parses port and constructs `url = f"http://{host}:{port}/"` before binding.
- `src/trade_trace/console/serve.py:567-576` checks port, builds app, prints banner, opens browser, then passes `port` to Uvicorn.
- Coordinator dry-run output:
  ```text
  {'host': '127.0.0.1', 'port': 0, 'url': 'http://127.0.0.1:0/', ...
   'banner': 'Trade Trace Console
  URL:           http://127.0.0.1:0/...'}
  ```

Failure mode / impact:
Operators following the documented port-conflict workaround receive an unusable URL/browser launch and confusing startup behavior.

## Steps to Reproduce
```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
from trade_trace.console.serve import _console_serve
print(_console_serve({'port':0,'_dry_run':True,'no_browser':True}, None))
PY
```

Duplicate check:
Compared against open issues and prior Console bugs during run `console-20260520T124742Z`. No open duplicate. This is distinct from the closed recent `/` and `/reports/*` 500 fix.

Suggested fix direction:
Prefer rejecting port 0 unless startup can bind/discover/report/open the actual socket port.

Validation:
`python3 -m pytest tests/contracts/test_console_serve.py -q`

Provenance:
Discovered by repo-bughunt candidate CAND-002 / lane-console-server-routes-security-002.
acceptance_criteria:
- Either port=0 is rejected with a clear validation error and docs stop recommending it, or serve startup reports/opens the actual OS-assigned port.
- A test covers console.serve port=0 behavior.
- Existing port-in-use exit-code behavior remains intact.
notes: Implemented safe port=0 rejection with VALIDATION_ERROR and updated docs to stop recommending --port=0; kept port-in-use exit-code behavior. Controller validation: combined focused console suite => 59 passed. Independent review: PASS.
close_reason: fixed

title: Console Logs page tail drops newest live entries when rotated logs exceed the requested tail
description:
Context:
Console `/logs` page and `logs_context` tailing across live and rotated operational log files.

Observed behavior:
With a current live log line and many older rotated lines, `logs_context(home, tail=5)` omits the live line and returns older rotated lines.

Expected behavior:
The Logs page should show the newest N operational log entries across live + rotated logs, including current live entries before older rotated content.

Evidence:
- `src/trade_trace/console/logs.py:41-53` builds `log_paths` as live file first, then rotated siblings.
- `src/trade_trace/console/logs.py:120-135` appends lines in that order, stops once `len(lines) >= tail`, then uses `lines[-tail:]`.
- `docs/CONSOLE.md:111` documents Logs as "Last N lines" with optional level filter.
- Coordinator repro output:
  ```text
  ['old15', 'old16', 'old17', 'old18', 'old19']
  ```
  from a temp log dir containing `trade-trace.log` with `current` plus `trade-trace.log.1` with 20 older entries and `tail=5`.

Failure mode / impact:
Operators can miss the most recent operational or security-relevant messages exactly when logs have rotated, making `/logs` misleading during debugging.

## Steps to Reproduce
```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import tempfile,json,os
from pathlib import Path
from trade_trace.console.logs import logs_context
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as d:
    p=Path(d); logdir=p/'logs'; logdir.mkdir()
    (logdir/'trade-trace.log').write_text(json.dumps({'level':'INFO','message':'current'})+'\n')
    (logdir/'trade-trace.log.1').write_text(''.join(json.dumps({'level':'INFO','message':f'old{i}'})+'\n' for i in range(20)))
    os.environ['TRADE_TRACE_LOG_DIR']=str(logdir)
    print([r['message'] for r in logs_context(home=p, tail=5)['rows']])
PY
```
Actual: `['old15', 'old16', 'old17', 'old18', 'old19']`; expected includes `current`.

Duplicate check:
Compared against open issues and prior Console/reporting bughunt themes during run `console-20260520T124742Z`. No open duplicate. Existing logs tests cover malformed/redaction/rotation only with large enough tail to include both files.

Suggested fix direction:
Read from newest source first in chronology-aware order, or otherwise keep the newest `tail` records across live + rotated files without dropping live entries.

Validation:
`python3 -m pytest tests/contracts/test_console_logs_page.py -q`

Provenance:
Discovered by repo-bughunt candidate CAND-001 / lane-console-server-routes-security-001.
acceptance_criteria:
- /logs and logs_context show the newest N entries across live + rotated logs.
- Regression test covers live file plus rotated file where rotated line count exceeds tail and asserts the live entry is present.
- Existing malformed-line, redaction, missing-dir, and no-write tests still pass.
notes: Implemented logs tail ordering via deque over rotated logs oldest-to-newest plus live last; added regression test for rotated tail overflow preserving current live row. Controller validation: python3 -m pytest tests/contracts/test_console_logs_page.py tests/contracts/test_console_serve.py tests/contracts/test_console_chart_bootstrap.py tests/contracts/test_console_shell.py tests/security/test_console_security_headers.py -q => 59 passed. Independent review: PASS.
close_reason: fixed

title: Focused Console bughunt 2026-05-20
description:
Context:
Focused bughunt over Trade Trace Console implementation at `/home/hermes/code/trade-trace/src/trade_trace/console` on 2026-05-20.

Scope:
- `src/trade_trace/console/**/*.py`
- `src/trade_trace/console/templates/**/*.html`
- `src/trade_trace/console/static/**/*`
- `docs/CONSOLE.md` where it defines Console behavior
- Console-related contract/integration/security/browser tests

Exclusions:
- Whole-repo exhaustive storage/CLI/security pass outside Console seams
- Implementing fixes
- Release/package publication work

Bug definition:
Only concrete failure modes were accepted: incorrect route/API/UI behavior, data/privacy/security/read-only contract risk, route 500/422, CLI/startup/deploy failure, docs contradiction that causes wrong operation, or test/build/package failure. Pure style/naming/speculative refactors were rejected.

Graph rule:
This epic is the narrative/root index, not executable remediation work. Membership is relation-based via `bd dep relate <epic-id> <bug-id>` plus labels. `bd children <epic-id>` may be empty by design.

Canonical navigation:

Counting rule:
Count bug findings separately from this narrative epic and any synthesis/final-verification task sharing `bughunt:console-20260520`.

Artifacts:

- Mutation audit: `/home/hermes/code/trade-trace/.hermes-bughunt/console-20260520T124742Z/mutation_audit.md`

Adding new findings:
New bug beads should include labels `bug,bughunt,bughunt:console-20260520,domain:<domain>`, line/probe evidence, duplicate rationale, acceptance criteria, validation command/gap, and a relation to this epic.

Close rule:
