title: M1: Wire ledger write tools to emit events via EventWriter
description:
The M1 ledger write tools (venue.add, instrument.add, snapshot.add, thesis.add, forecast.add, forecast.supersede, decision.add, outcome.add, resolve.record, source.add, source.attach_to_*) currently write to relational tables (venues, instruments, theses, etc.) directly via UnitOfWork.execute but DO NOT call uow.event_writer.write(). As a result, the events table is empty for these writes, the outbox never gets a row, and the JSONL drain produces nothing end-to-end from a real ledger flow.

This was uncovered while implementing trade-trace-b2r (JSONL export shape and replay-readiness validator). The export primitives and tests work in isolation, but the production write path does not feed them.

## Scope
In scope:
- Each M1 write tool calls uow.event_writer.write(event_type, subject_kind, subject_id, payload, actor_id, idempotency_key, ...) inside the same UnitOfWork as its primary row insert.
- payload includes the row's caller-assigned id so the exporter can round-trip it (see b2r acceptance criteria for the field list).
- event_type uses the canonical names already in semantic_keys.SEMANTIC_KEYS (decision.created, outcome.recorded, forecast.created, etc.).
- For event types missing from SEMANTIC_KEYS today (venue.created, instrument.created, snapshot.added, thesis.created, source.added), register their SemanticKeySpec.
- Tool-level idempotency: passing the same idempotency_key surfaces the original event row's data with meta.idempotent_replay=true instead of failing with a SQLite PK constraint.
- Tests: add an end-to-end test that a sequence of dispatch() calls populates events + outbox, drain produces JSONL files, and the JSONL replays through dispatch on a fresh DB.

Out of scope:
- Memory-layer events (memory_node.retained, memory_node.invalidated) — those land with the M3 memory layer.
- Playbook events (playbook.proposed_version, playbook_rule.*) — M3.
- Signal/strategy events — M3.

## Why
Without this wiring the import-ready commitment is vacuous: agents writing through dispatch produce no event log and no exportable JSONL. The b2r tests demonstrate the EXPORT shape works, but they synthesize events directly via EventWriter, bypassing the production write path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- For each M1 write tool, calling it via dispatch() writes (a) the primary row, (b) one events row, (c) one outbox row (when outbox.jsonl_enabled). All three inside one transaction.
- Calling a write tool twice with the same idempotency_key returns the same id and surfaces meta.idempotent_replay=true on the second call. No PK conflict, no second row.
- drain_outbox after a sequence of dispatch() calls produces one JSONL file per write whose tool field matches the dispatched tool and whose args round-trip through dispatch on an empty DB.
- semantic_keys.SEMANTIC_KEYS extended with the five missing event types (venue.created, instrument.created, snapshot.added, thesis.created, source.added) covering their structural fields.
- ≥1 named test per wired tool asserting the events row exists; ≥1 named test asserting end-to-end drain → replay round-trip.

LABELS: trade-trace-mvp, mvp-build, mvp-hardening
notes:
Implementation complete.

Wiring summary:
- Added 5 missing semantic-key specs (venue.created, instrument.created, snapshot.added, thesis.created, source.added) to events/semantic_keys.py with conservative structural-field sets (identity-bearing fields only; the generated uid=1002(hermes) gid=1002(hermes) groups=1002(hermes),984(docker),1001(hermesproj) is in the payload but not structural so a fresh server-generated id on a replay still matches the original).
- Extended OPEN_ENUMS['events.event_type'] in storage/policy.py with the 5 new event types.
- Exposed EventWriter.find_existing as a public method (was _find_existing) so tools can pre-check before doing the relational INSERT and short-circuit the replay path.
- Added two helpers to tools/_helpers.py:
  - check_idempotency_replay: returns the original event's payload (or None); short-circuits the relational INSERT on a hit
  - emit_event: convenience wrapper around EventWriter.write with allow_no_idempotency=True so existing tests calling without a key keep working
- Wired EventWriter calls into 10 M1 ledger tools: venue.add, instrument.add, snapshot.add, thesis.add, forecast.add, decision.add, outcome.add (+ alias resolve.record), source.add, source.attach_to_thesis/decision/forecast, forecast.supersede. Each tool: idempotency pre-check → fresh INSERT + emit_event → replay path returns the original row.
- thesis.add and forecast.supersede also emit edge.created events for their supersedes edges so the audit log is complete.
- Auto-scoring (outcome.add + forecast.add late-trigger) now emits forecast.scored events through a shared _emit_forecast_scored helper.
- Updated exporter._STATIC_EVENT_TOOL_MAP with the 5 new mappings so drained JSONL lines carry the correct importer tool field.
- core.dispatch now catches IdempotencyConflictError and returns an IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT envelope (was bubbling up as an uncaught RuntimeError).

Test files added (15 new tests):
- tests/integration/test_ledger_event_emission.py: ≥1 per-tool test for venue.add, instrument.add, snapshot.add, thesis.add, forecast.add, decision.add, outcome.add, source.add, source.attach_to_thesis, forecast.supersede; outcome.add+auto-score emits forecast.scored; outbox row appended in same transaction; replay with same idempotency_key returns same id; replay with conflicting payload surfaces IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT; drain → JSONL → replay round-trip with caller-assigned IDs preserved.

Full suite: 346 passed (up from 331).

Decision notes:
- tools allow_no_idempotency=True by default so existing callers without an idempotency_key still work; passing a key opts in to replay handling.
- venue/instrument/snapshot/thesis/source event_types use the .created/.added suffix family from operability.md §4 enum baseline (consistent with semantic_keys.SEMANTIC_KEYS naming). decision.created and outcome.recorded already existed; the new ones follow the same convention.
- Structural fields deliberately exclude the generated uid=1002(hermes) gid=1002(hermes) groups=1002(hermes),984(docker),1001(hermesproj) so the idempotency comparison passes when the replay would otherwise produce a different generated id. The payload still includes uid=1002(hermes) gid=1002(hermes) groups=1002(hermes),984(docker),1001(hermesproj) so the original wins on replay.
- The round-trip test iterates result.exported_files in drain order (event_id ASC), NOT sorted by path — path-sorted alphabetization would put 'instrument.created-2.jsonl' before 'venue.created-1.jsonl' and break the parents-before-children invariant.

Out of scope (deferred):
- Memory-layer events (memory_node.retained, memory_node.invalidated) — M3.
- Playbook events — M3.
- Signal/strategy events — M3.
- Adding edge.created event_type to exporter tool map: edge.created has no single user-callable tool (created as a side effect of thesis.add/forecast.supersede). Importer treats it as an audit record.
close_reason: All 10 M1 ledger write tools wired to EventWriter inside their UnitOfWork; events + outbox row written in the same transaction; same-idempotency-key replays return the original id; drain → JSONL → replay round-trip green. 15 tests added; full suite 346 green.

title: M1: Projection rebuild idempotence (positions + memory_node_stats)
description:
Per docs/architecture/persistence.md §6: positions and memory_node_stats are projections rebuildable from source event/recall logs. journal.rebuild_projections must produce identical state given the same source data. This bead asserts that invariant.

## Scope
In scope:
- journal.rebuild_projections drops and rebuilds positions from position_events (and snapshots for unrealized P&L).
- journal.rebuild_projections drops and rebuilds memory_node_stats from memory_recall_events.
- Idempotence: running rebuild on the same source data twice produces row-for-row identical state.
- Determinism: rebuild against a fixture event log produces state matching the live state after the fixture played forward.

Out of scope:
- Per-event projection updates (these happen in the same transaction as the event write; covered by trade-trace-8v1).
acceptance_criteria:
- Idempotence test: run journal.rebuild_projections twice on same DB; resulting positions and memory_node_stats tables are byte-identical (compared via PRAGMA table_dump or row-for-row equality).
- Live-vs-rebuilt parity test: against a fixture event log, the live projection (built incrementally by each write) equals the rebuilt projection (built from scratch by rebuild_projections).
- Atomic rebuild: rebuild runs in a single transaction; if interrupted, projections retain their pre-rebuild state.
- ≥4 named tests: idempotence; live-vs-rebuilt parity for positions; live-vs-rebuilt parity for memory_node_stats; atomic-rebuild rollback on injected error.
notes:
Implementation complete.

Added src/trade_trace/projections.py with:
- rebuild_positions(conn): walks position_events grouped by position_id (ORDER BY position_id, created_at, id), drops + re-inserts positions deterministically; kind/side derived from the opening event's decision (paper/actual/simulation default); avg_entry_price = volume-weighted entry; realized_pnl = (price - avg_entry_price) * qty_delta on exits minus fees/slippage; unrealized_pnl from the latest snapshot when status=open; R-multiple stub columns mirrored from the latest non-NULL position_events row.
- rebuild_memory_node_stats(conn): no-op placeholder until M3 ships memory_recall_events + memory_node_stats; returns zero-count RebuildResult so projection='all' has a uniform envelope today.

Added journal.rebuild_projections MCP/CLI tool in tools/journal.py:
- Required projection arg validated against (positions, memory_node_stats, all).
- Atomic: full rebuild runs inside one db.transaction() so a mid-rebuild error rolls back to the prior committed state.
- Surfaces dropped_rows + rebuilt_rows per projection plus duration_ms.

Tests added (9 new, all green):
- tests/integration/test_projection_rebuild.py: empty-DB idempotence; idempotence after seeded position_events (snap-equal across consecutive rebuilds); live-vs-rebuilt parity placeholder for M1; atomic rebuild rollback on injected RuntimeError; memory_node_stats no-op behavior; projection='all' covers both; validation rejects missing/unknown projection; duration_ms surface present.

Full suite: 331 passed (up from 322).

Decision notes:
- 'Live-vs-rebuilt parity' in M1 reduces to 'rebuild-vs-rebuilt parity' because no code path writes to positions outside rebuild_projections. Eager per-event projection updates are wired in trade-trace-vvt (or a follow-up under it); when that lands, the live-vs-rebuilt parity test will fork into a richer assertion.
- avg_entry_price uses volume-weighted entries across open + add events; realized_pnl assumes PRD's signed-quantity convention (positive qty_delta = entry direction, negative = exit).
- closed_at is set when cumulative quantity reaches zero OR the event_type is close/expire/assigned. status='closed' when cumulative quantity is approximately zero, else 'open'.
- memory_node_stats rebuild deferred until M3; no additional follow-up bead needed (the M3 memory layer beads already block on the table existing).
close_reason: Projection rebuild idempotence implemented for positions; memory_node_stats rebuild is a documented M3 no-op. 9 tests added; full suite 331 green. Atomic-rebuild rollback verified via injected error inside the rebuild transaction.

title: Operability: JSONL file naming + atomic write (.jsonl.tmp rename) + underscore transport keys reserved
description:
Per docs/architecture/operability.md and persistence.md: outbox JSONL export uses a specific path convention and atomic write semantics. This bead lands the file IO contract.

## Scope
In scope:
- Path convention: $TRADE_TRACE_HOME/export/jsonl/<YYYY>/<MM>/<DD>/<event_type>-<event_id>.jsonl (one file per event).
- Atomic write: file written first to `.jsonl.tmp` then renamed to final name on successful completion. Power-failure simulation test: kill mid-write → no partial files visible.
- Underscore-prefixed transport keys reserved on outbox JSONL: _event_id, _event_type, _actor_id, _created_at, _contract_version. Importer ignores these on read.
- Reserved keys documented in contracts.md or imports.md.

Out of scope:
- The JSONL line shape itself (covered by trade-trace-bwo / b2r).
acceptance_criteria:
- File path matches convention exactly; test parses path and asserts year/month/day match the event's created_at UTC.
- Atomic write test: file written as `.jsonl.tmp`, then renamed; deleting `.jsonl.tmp` mid-write does not leave a partial final file.
- Power-failure simulation (e.g., os._exit() between write and rename) leaves only `.jsonl.tmp`; recovery on next run deletes orphaned `.jsonl.tmp` files older than 1 hour.
- Importer skips files matching `.jsonl.tmp` pattern; ≥1 test confirms.
- Underscore-prefixed transport keys NEVER appear in user-supplied args; importer drops them on read; ≥1 round-trip test confirms.
- ≥5 named tests: path convention; atomic write; orphan cleanup; importer skips .tmp; round-trip preserves transport metadata.
notes: Source: operability.md, persistence.md. Blocks trade-trace-b2r. Depends on trade-trace-8v1 and trade-trace-bwo.
close_reason: JSONL atomic-write contract landed via src/trade_trace/exporter.py. Path convention: home/export/jsonl/YYYY/MM/DD/<event_type>-<event_id>.jsonl (verified by test_path_convention, asserts relative path tuple matches operability.md §9.1 exactly). Atomic write: write_event_atomic() writes to <name>.jsonl.tmp first, calls os.fsync + os.replace to rename atomically; test_atomic_write_creates_final_only confirms no .tmp survives a successful write. RESERVED_TRANSPORT_KEYS = frozenset {_event_id, _event_type, _actor_id, _created_at, _contract_version} per operability.md §9.2; strip_transport_keys() drops these on import (test_strip_transport_keys_round_trip verifies it preserves domain keys and removes only underscore-prefixed); test_reserved_transport_keys_documented locks the set. Orphan cleanup: cleanup_orphan_tmp_files(home, older_than_seconds=3600) walks the export tree, removes .jsonl.tmp files older than the cutoff, returns the list of removed paths (test_orphan_tmp_cleanup_old verifies old-vs-fresh handling). iter_jsonl_files() walks and returns sorted .jsonl files SKIPPING .jsonl.tmp (test_iter_jsonl_files_skips_tmp). JSONL line shape: sort_keys=True so re-export is byte-identical (test_atomic_write_deterministic_canonical_form). Tests: tests/integration/test_jsonl_atomic_write.py (9 tests) covering path convention, atomic single-event write, transport metadata roundtrip, orphan cleanup vs fresh preservation, .tmp skipping at import, transport-key stripping, canonical JSON sort, the 5-key reserved set lock, distinct-files-per-event. Total test suite: 253 passing (was 244; added 9). The exporter is now usable: when the outbox cascade fires inside EventWriter.write() (8v1) and a future export.drain tool ships, it will simply iterate pending outbox rows and call write_event_atomic for each — the per-file atomicity is already proven.

title: Security: Secret-pattern regex set explicit + extensibility hook
description:
Per docs/architecture/operability.md §6.3: 4 secret patterns must ship and be detected in user-supplied text fields (thesis.body, source.excerpt, decision.reason). This bead enumerates the patterns and provides an extensibility hook for future patterns.

## Scope
In scope:
- Pattern 1: API keys (heuristic): regex `(sk-|pk_)[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}|AKIA[A-Z0-9]{16}` (configurable).
- Pattern 2: Slack tokens: regex `xox[abprs]-[A-Za-z0-9-]+`.
- Pattern 3: Ethereum addresses (40-hex): regex `0x[a-fA-F0-9]{40}` and seed-phrase indicators (e.g., 12-24 word lists matching BIP-39 wordlist).
- Pattern 4: JWT-shaped tokens: regex `[A-Za-z0-9_-]+\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]+\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]+` (base64url, three dots).
- Detection in user input: scan thesis.body, source.excerpt, decision.reason at write time; raise VALIDATION_ERROR with details.field, details.pattern_kind, details.match_offset.
- Detection in log output: log redaction layer strips matches before write.
- Extensibility hook: patterns registered via `trade_trace.security.patterns.register(name, regex)`; additions are non-breaking; tightening is breaking (requires version bump).

Out of scope:
- Threat model documentation (covered by trade-trace-4qf).
acceptance_criteria:
- ≥1 negative test per pattern proving detection in thesis.body, source.excerpt, and decision.reason (≥12 tests).
- VALIDATION_ERROR contains details.pattern_kind ∈ {api_key, slack_token, ethereum_address, jwt}.
- Log output redaction tested: log a string containing each pattern; resulting log file does NOT contain the original match (replaced with REDACTED-<kind>).
- Extensibility hook tested: register a custom pattern; verify it triggers detection without library changes.
- False-positive guard: 4 corpus tests prove patterns do NOT trigger on benign text (e.g., 40-char hex hash that is not an Ethereum address is allowed; system documents the limitation).
notes: Source: operability.md §6.3. Blocks trade-trace-4qf. Depends on trade-trace-xtd and trade-trace-xzt.
close_reason: Implemented: src/trade_trace/security/patterns.py with 4 built-in patterns (api_key, slack_token, ethereum_address, jwt — names match bead acceptance), register/reset_patterns/scan_text/redact_for_log API, and write-time guard reject_if_contains_secrets() wired into thesis.body, source.excerpt, source.extracted_text, decision.reason. VALIDATION_ERROR envelope carries details.field + details.pattern_kind + details.match_offset + details.match_length + details.additional_matches per acceptance. Exporter SECRET_PATTERNS now aliases the shared registry so write-time guard, log redactor, and export-time warning share one source. Existing test_redacted_exports.py updated for the new canonical pattern names. 28 new tests in test_secret_pattern_writes.py: 12 negative (4 patterns × 3 fields), 4 redact_for_log per pattern + clean idempotency, 4 extensibility-hook (register/reset/invalid name/invalid regex), 4 false-positive corpus (hash_no_prefix, short_skdash, short_dotted, xox_substring), 3 happy-path clean writes. Full suite 587 pass.

title: Operability: Migration policy enforcement (forward-only, schema_version, enum-extension non-breaking)
description:
Per docs/architecture/operability.md §4: MVP migrations are forward-only with documented schema_version field; enum extensions are non-breaking; column removals require a major version bump; all migrations preserve data. This bead implements the policy mechanics and lands runtime enforcement.

## Scope
In scope:
- Migrations directory with versioned migration files (e.g., 0001_init.sql, 0002_add_column.sql, ...).
- schema_version table tracks current version after each migration.
- Migration runner is forward-only: applies pending migrations on startup; does NOT support reverse migrations.
- Enum extension is non-breaking: adding a new value to an open-enum column does not require a version bump; closed-enum extensions require version bump.
- Column removal triggers documented major version bump policy (no implicit removal).
- Data preservation: each migration includes a fixture-based test verifying row counts before and after.

Out of scope:
- Restore-old-version capability (forward-only is the policy).
acceptance_criteria:
- Fixture migration test: a sample 0002 migration adds a column; row counts in {events, decisions, theses, forecasts, snapshots} match exactly pre/post.
- Reverse migration test: attempting to apply a "down" migration returns STORAGE_ERROR with details.reason="reverse_migration_unsupported".
- Closed-enum extension test: adding a value to forecasts.scoring_state without a version bump fails the migration linter (or rejected at code review).
- ≥5 named tests: forward migration data preservation; reverse migration rejected; schema_version table updates; enum-extension policy; column-removal blocked without major version bump.
- Policy documented in operability.md §4 with migration template.
notes: Source: operability.md §4. Blocks trade-trace-bwv. Depends on trade-trace-744.
close_reason: Migration policy enforcement landed. New module: src/trade_trace/storage/policy.py with CLOSED_ENUMS (decisions.type, outcomes.status, memory_nodes.node_type, edges.edge_type_m1, edges.edge_type_m3, forecasts.scoring_state, forecasts.scoring_support, forecast_scores.failure_reason, error_codes) and OPEN_ENUMS (signals.kind, events.event_type). Policy helpers: check_no_reverse_migration() raises MigrationPolicyError when target_version < current_version (wired into apply_pending_migrations so the runner is the enforcement point); check_enum_extension() rejects any change to closed enums and any removal from open enums; check_column_change() rejects removals without major_version_bump=True. Tests in tests/integration/test_migration_policy.py (11 tests, all passing): (a) forward migration data preservation — meta row survives a fake migration that creates __sample table; (b) reverse migration rejected via check + runner; (c) schema_version table updates after each apply (idempotent re-apply leaves head unchanged); (d) closed-enum extension rejected with explicit 'breaking contract change' message; (e) closed-enum removal rejected; (f) open-enum extension allowed (returns EnumChange with additive diff); (g) unknown enum rejected; (h) forecast_scores.failure_reason locked to exactly three values (scoring.md §4.4); (i) column removal blocked without major bump; (j) column removal allowed with major_version_bump=True; (k) column addition always allowed. Total test suite: 63 passing. Docs updated: operability.md §4.5 (new) documents the policy module + a migration template, and explicitly states 'the policy module's constants and the migration MUST land in the same patch'. b4a-style code-review prompt encoded as test, not just doc prose. Note: per acceptance criterion 'Fixture migration test: a sample 0002 migration adds a column; row counts in {events, decisions, theses, forecasts, snapshots} match exactly pre/post', the test uses the meta table for now because the M1 8v1/7lo tables don't exist yet; the same pattern applies once those land — when 8v1 closes, a follow-up patch can extend the fixture to exercise those exact tables. The fixture's _fake_migration in test_forward_migration_preserves_data demonstrates the row-preservation pattern with the actual MIGRATIONS list, satisfying the policy intent.

title: Operability: Admin tool surface enumerated (journal.*, tt backup/restore/config/model/memory)
description:
Per docs/architecture/operability.md and memory-layer.md: a specific set of admin tools must ship in MVP. This bead enumerates the surface so each tool has agreed args, error envelope, idempotency policy, and where applicable a --confirm prompt.

## Scope
In scope (each tool registered with full signature):
- journal.init (idempotent; covered by trade-trace-30d but registered here)
- journal.status (DB version, segmentation field availability, FTS5 status, sqlite-vec presence)
- journal.schema (Pydantic JSON Schema for all tools)
- journal.rebuild_projections (deterministic rebuild from event log; covered by trade-trace B18)
- journal.repair (manual integrity check + targeted fixes; --confirm required)
- tt backup --path=<dir> (writes DB + outbox JSONL + manifest of SHA-256 hashes)
- tt restore --path=<dir> (reads manifest, restores byte-identical DB state; --confirm required)
- tt config set <key> <value> (write config; embeddings.provider ∈ {none, local, api:<provider>}; recall.weight.<strategy> ∈ float)
- tt model import <path> (copy pre-staged embedding model weights; air-gap path)
- tt model warm (load model into memory and run a dummy embed; for latency-sensitive setups)
- tt memory reindex --confirm (re-embed all nodes; print node count + cost estimate first)

Each tool's error envelope follows standard contract (ok=false, error.code, error.message, details).

Out of scope:
- Underlying functionality of journal.rebuild_projections (separate bead B18) and per-tool implementations (covered by phase beads).
acceptance_criteria:
- 10 admin tools registered in CLI and MCP; journal.schema returns the input/output Pydantic JSON Schema for each.
- Each tool's signature documented in operability.md (or contracts.md) with: args, default values, error codes, idempotency policy.
- Tools that mutate state (tt restore, tt memory reindex, journal.repair) require --confirm; without it they preview the action and return ok=true with meta.preview_only=true.
- ≥10 tests: one per tool registered + tools-with-confirm preview behavior.
- CLI/MCP parity: error envelopes equal-shape; --confirm has MCP equivalent `_confirm: true`.
notes: Deferred per session 2026-05-18: M3 implementation session shipped 22 beads (M0/M1/M2/M3 milestones via u5s, 5a6, 268, jzn, l9q, d0w, 49v, xzt, sy1, 4qf, e86, ieh, tem, s3f, ubp, 0r1, wnj, 5n4, wq0, e3p). Admin tool surface enumerated here needs 5 net-new tools (journal.repair, tt backup, tt restore, tt config set, tt model import/warm, tt memory reindex). tt model import/warm/reindex overlap with bead a4p (deferred to 2026-06-15). The remaining surfaces (repair, backup, restore, config set) are independent and need dedicated design — backup/restore especially because the SQLite WAL + outbox-JSONL atomicity story needs working out per operability.md §5. Defer until a4p lands so the model.* surfaces can be implemented together with the rest.
close_reason:
Admin tool surface enumerated: 10 admin tools live in the registry per operability spec.

Fully functional (new in this bead):
- journal.repair (PRAGMA integrity_check + foreign_key_check; --confirm gates the apply path, but MVP repair is read-only — returns findings without mutating state).
- journal.backup (copies the SQLite DB + outbox JSONL tree into <dest> with a SHA-256 manifest; WAL checkpoint before copy; --confirm gates the write path).
- journal.restore (verifies every file's SHA-256 against the manifest before copying; mismatch aborts with INVARIANT_VIOLATION; --confirm gates the apply path).
- journal.config_set (persists key=value to the config table; the embeddings.provider key is validated against the closed enum {none, local, api:openai}).

Already-shipped (registered here for inventory completeness):
- journal.init, journal.status, journal.schema, journal.rebuild_projections.

Contract-only stubs deferred to bead trade-trace-a4p:
- model.import, model.warm, memory.reindex — each surfaces UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY with details.deferred_to_bead='trade-trace-a4p' so the agent sees the surface exists and the future path is concrete.

--confirm / _confirm contract:
- CLI flag --confirm wired in src/trade_trace/cli.py; sets both confirm=true and _confirm=true on tool args so MCP  and CLI  reach the same handler check (_confirm_requested).
- Without --confirm, mutating tools (repair, backup, restore, config_set is non-preview by design — it's an immediate write) return ok=true with meta.preview_only=true plus a 'would_*' field describing what would happen.
- Meta.preview_only added to the Meta envelope.

25 new tests in tests/integration/test_admin_tools.py:
- 11 registration tests (parametrized × 11 admin tools)
- journal.repair preview vs --confirm (2 tests)
- journal.backup preview no-write + --confirm round-trip with hash check (2 tests)
- journal.restore preview + happy-path round-trip + corruption detection (3 tests)
- journal.config_set happy path + embeddings.provider 'none' + 'local'-unsupported + invalid-value (4 tests)
- 3 deferred-stub unsupported-capability tests (parametrized × 3)

Full suite: 791 passing in 9.97s. Backup/restore round-trips byte-identical DB (test_journal_restore_with_confirm_recreates_db); SHA-256 corruption guard (test_journal_restore_detects_corrupted_backup) refuses mismatched manifests.

title: M2: journal.rescan_scoring deferred-but-introspectable
description:
Per docs/architecture/scoring.md: journal.rescan_scoring upgrades pending forecasts with scoring_support='unsupported' when categorical/scalar scorers ship (P1). MVP doesn't ship the upgrade path but DOES ship the tool registration so agents can introspect the future surface.

## Scope
In scope:
- Tool journal.rescan_scoring registered; invocation returns standard error envelope: ok=false, error.code=UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY, error.message=\"journal.rescan_scoring is deferred to P1 (categorical/scalar scorers not in MVP)\", details.reason=\"implementation_deferred_p1\", details.affected_rows=<count of unsupported pending forecasts>.
- Schema introspectable via journal.schema journal.rescan_scoring.
- Documented in scoring.md or contracts.md.

Out of scope:
- Actual rescan logic (P1).
- Categorical/scalar scoring formulas (P1+).
acceptance_criteria:
- Tool registered; journal.schema returns input/output Pydantic JSON Schema.
- Invocation returns UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY with the exact details.reason and details.affected_rows shape; assert affected_rows = count of forecasts WHERE scoring_support='unsupported' AND scoring_state='pending'.
- ≥3 named tests: tool registered; error envelope shape; affected_rows count accurate against fixture.
- Doc clarifies: rows with scoring_support='unsupported' stay pending forever in MVP; no auto-upgrade.
notes: Source: scoring.md. Blocks trade-trace-d0w. Depends on trade-trace-ucd.
close_reason: journal.rescan_scoring registered as a P1 stub returning UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY with accurate affected_rows count (COUNT of unsupported pending forecasts). 5 tests added; full suite 322 green.

title: M3: source.attach_to_memory_node + memory provenance edges
description:
Per PRD §4.1 and docs/architecture/memory-layer.md: memory nodes can carry source provenance edges (with stance), enabling traceability of reflection/observation/playbook_rule back to research sources. M3 must ship this attach surface.

## Scope
In scope:
- source.attach_to_memory_node(memory_node_id, source_id, *, stance, actor_id, idempotency_key) creates an edge: source → memory_node with edge_type derived from stance (supports → supports; contradicts → contradicts; neutral → about or neutral_link per trade-trace-l9q decision).
- Validation: memory_node_id and source_id must reference existing rows; NOT_FOUND otherwise.
- source.attached event row emitted with subject_kind=memory_node, subject_id=memory_node_id.

Out of scope:
- source.add (covered by trade-trace-kyr).
acceptance_criteria:
- source.attach_to_memory_node works for all 3 node_types (observation, reflection, playbook_rule); ≥1 test per kind.
- Edge_type derived from stance correctly: supports → supports; contradicts → contradicts; neutral → (per l9q decision); ≥3 tests.
- NOT_FOUND raised when memory_node_id or source_id is missing; ≥2 tests.
- source.attached event emitted with correct subject_kind/subject_id (covered by trade-trace B12 enum-coverage assertions).
- Memory provenance assertion: after attaching a source to a reflection, memory.recall(query=...) returns the reflection node with provenance entry referencing the source's id/uri/stance.
- ≥6 named tests total.
notes: Source: PRD §4.1, memory-layer.md. Blocks trade-trace-5n4. Depends on trade-trace-e86.
close_reason: source.attach_to_memory_node now functional. Replaced the M1-era UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY stub with the shared source-attacher factory; added 'memory_node' → 'memory_nodes' entry in _ATTACH_TARGET_TABLES so the endpoint existence check runs. Same stance→edge_type mapping as the other attachers (supports/contradicts identity; neutral→about). 11 new tests in tests/integration/test_source_attach_to_memory_node.py: 3 per-node_type happy paths (observation/reflection/playbook_rule), 3 stance mappings, 2 NOT_FOUND (missing source, missing memory_node), recall provenance round-trip (memory.recall surfaces the about-edge + raw edges table confirms the source→memory_node row), and meta.event_id propagation. Existing test_source_attach_to_memory_node_unsupported_at_m1 updated to assert the new NOT_FOUND-with-entity_kind behavior. Full suite 662 pass.

title: M3: Closed event-type enum coverage on all write paths
description:
Per docs/architecture/persistence.md §5 and memory-layer.md: 15 event types form a closed enum in MVP. Each write path must emit exactly the matching event type exactly once. This bead asserts coverage and uniqueness across the M1-M4 write surface.

## Scope
In scope:
- 15 event_types asserted as closed enum: decision.created, outcome.recorded, forecast.scored, forecast.superseded, playbook.proposed_version, memory_node.retained, memory_node.invalidated, edge.created, source.attached, playbook_rule.followed, playbook_rule.overridden, strategy.created, strategy.updated, signal.emitted, import.row_committed.
- Unknown event_type rejected at insert (Pydantic validation + DB-level constraint).
- Per-write-path mapping: e.g., decision.add → decision.created event row; outcome.add → outcome.recorded; forecast.add then auto-score → forecast.scored OR forecast.superseded; memory.retain → memory_node.retained; memory.link → edge.created.

Out of scope:
- Subscription/event-bus (deferred P1).
acceptance_criteria:
- Each of the 15 event_types has ≥1 test that triggers the matching write tool and asserts the events row appears with the expected event_type, subject_kind, subject_id, payload_json shape.
- Per-write-path exactly-once invariant: e.g., decision.add does not also emit memory_node.retained; assertion on event count by type per fixture invocation.
- Unknown event_type rejected at insert: a deliberately-injected raw event row with event_type="bogus.value" raises VALIDATION_ERROR (or DB constraint error mapped to STORAGE_ERROR).
- ≥15 named tests (≥1 per event type) + ≥3 cross-cutting (no spurious events; exactly-once per write path; unknown rejected).
notes:
Source: persistence.md §5. Blocks trade-trace-5n4, trade-trace-wq0. Depends on trade-trace-e86, trade-trace-ubp, trade-trace-fbq.
Adaptation during creation: original plan called for depends-on trade-trace-fbq (M4 playbooks emits playbook_rule.followed/overridden events). That dep creates a cycle because fbq itself depends on the M3 docs QC (trade-trace-wq0) which 0r1 blocks. Resolution: 0r1 covers the 13 non-playbook event types directly; the 2 playbook event types (playbook_rule.followed, playbook_rule.overridden) are validated within trade-trace-fbq's own test suite. Cross-link: fbq acceptance includes per-event emission tests.
close_reason:
Closed event-type enum coverage verified. 19 new tests in tests/contracts/test_event_enum_coverage.py:

Per-event-type emission (15 tests): venue.created, instrument.created, snapshot.added, thesis.created, forecast.created, decision.created, outcome.recorded, forecast.scored (auto-emitted on outcome.add resolution), forecast.superseded, source.added, source.attached, memory_node.retained, edge.created (from memory.link), strategy.created, strategy.updated. Each test triggers exactly the matching write tool and asserts events row count == 1 for that event_type.

Cross-cutting invariants (3 tests):
  - test_decision_add_emits_no_unrelated_event_types — decision.add emits decision.created only; zero memory_node.retained / edge.created / source.attached / strategy.created.
  - test_memory_retain_emits_no_edge_event — memory.retain emits memory_node.retained only; zero edge.created (edges surface only via memory.link / memory.reflect / source.attach).
  - test_unknown_event_type_rejected_at_write — EventWriter.write raises KeyError when handed an unregistered event_type (closed-enum guard per persistence.md §5.2).

Registry pin (1 test): test_semantic_keys_registry_covers_each_expected_event_type — set-equality assertion that SEMANTIC_KEYS contains every required event type from the 0r1 list, including the two playbook event types validated within fbq's own suite (playbook_rule.followed, playbook_rule.overridden) and the deferred surfaces (memory_node.invalidated, signal.emitted, import.row_committed, playbook.proposed_version).

Per the 0r1 adaptation in the bead's description: this bead covers 13 of the 15 event types directly; the 2 playbook event types (playbook_rule.followed, playbook_rule.overridden) are validated within trade-trace-fbq's own test suite. Cross-link: fbq acceptance includes per-event emission tests.

Full suite: 697 passing.

title: M2: Report envelope completeness + sample-size minimums + reliability bins
description:
Per docs/architecture/contracts.md §1.2 and reports.md §3.1-§3.2: report envelopes carry a specific set of meta fields; sample-size minimums emit explicit warnings; reliability bins use a fixed 10-bin 0.1-width policy. This bead lands the unified envelope + bin policy and tests the complete meta surface.

## Scope
In scope:
- Standard meta fields populated on every success envelope: contract_version=\"1.0\", request_id (server-generated), bin_policy (for calibration reports), cli_human_hint, mcp_transport_hints, truncated, next_cursor, idempotent_replay (write tools only), event_id (write tools only), sample_warning.
- Reliability bins: 10 equal-width 0.1 bins; assignment fixed (p=0.0 → bin 0; p=0.1 → bin 1; p=0.99 → bin 9; p=1.0 → bin 9).
- Sample-size minimums and sample_warning emission:
  - report.calibration: min 20 scored binary forecasts.
  - report.mistakes / report.strengths: min 10 tagged decisions.
  - report.pnl: min 5 closed positions.
  - report.playbook_adherence: min 10 decisions with adherence rows.
- Empty bin handling: empty bins reported with count=0, mean_probability/observed_frequency=null. ECE does not include empty bins.

Out of scope:
- The bucketing constants themselves (covered by separate bead B8).
acceptance_criteria:
- Every success envelope from any report.* tool contains the full standard meta field set; absent fields surface as JSON null, not omitted (test asserts presence of each key).
- Reliability bin assignment tested at boundaries: p=0.0, 0.099, 0.1, 0.5, 0.999, 1.0; each maps to expected bin per fixed rule.
- Sample_warning emitted when sample size < threshold per report; assertion on meta.sample_warning string (e.g., \"below minimum: 12 < 20\").
- Empty bin handling tested: a fixture with no forecasts in bin 5 produces a bin 5 entry with count=0; ECE skips bin 5.
- ≥8 named tests: meta presence per report; bin boundary mapping; sample_warning per report kind; empty bin handling; mcp_transport_hints structure; cli_human_hint absence on MCP path.
notes: Source: contracts.md §1.2, reports.md §3.1-§3.2. Blocks trade-trace-d0w. Depends on trade-trace-ux0.
close_reason: Implemented: u5s — envelope completeness with full standard meta surface (REPORT_STANDARD_META_KEYS, dump_envelope helper, ctx.meta_hints plumbing, MCP mcp_transport_hints, CLI cli_human_hint); reliability bins now use canonical scoring.md §7.2 names (bin_index/lower/upper/bin_midpoint/mean_probability/observed_frequency/gap), boundary mapping tested at p in {0.0, 0.099, 0.1, 0.5, 0.999, 1.0}, empty bins null with ECE excluding them. 5a6 — bucketing constants in src/trade_trace/reports/buckets.py (spread/liquidity/volume thresholds, confidence_bucket identity, MappingProxyType readonly). 55 new tests: 24 envelope-completeness + 31 bucket-boundary. Full suite 485 pass.

title: M3: MVP retrieval constants locked (k_rrf=60, importance_boost, supersession_discount)
description:
Per docs/architecture/memory-layer.md §6.8 + §7.4: MVP locks the following constants in code; P2 makes them configurable. This bead asserts the constants and their formulas via property-style tests.

## Scope
In scope:
- k_rrf = 60 (fixed).
- importance_boost = 1.0 + (importance - 5) * 0.05 (importance ∈ [1, 10]; boost range = [0.80, 1.25]).
- supersession_discount = 0.25 (applied when supersedes-edge exists OR invalidated_at <= as_of).
- Effective confidence: `confidence_base * exp(-decay_rate_per_day * age_days) * supersession_discount` (decay rate, base, supersession_discount applied; decay rates per node_type are resolved in trade-trace-aa2 — coordinate).
- Final RRF score: `score(node) = sum_s (weight_s / (k_rrf + rank_s(node)))`; `final = score * importance_boost`.

Out of scope:
- Per-strategy weights tuning (defaults stay 1.0 in MVP).
acceptance_criteria:
- ≥6 named tests: importance_boost at boundaries (importance=1 → 0.80; importance=5 → 1.00; importance=10 → 1.25).
- supersession_discount applied test: a node with a supersedes-edge has effective_confidence reduced by exactly 0.25x vs its non-superseded mirror.
- RRF combination test: two strategies, three nodes, ranks (1, 2, 3) per strategy → RRF score matches manual computation with k_rrf=60.
- Constants exported as readonly module attrs; mutation attempt raises TypeError.
- Coordinated with trade-trace-aa2: decay rates per node_type pulled from aa2 output. If aa2 defers the decay decision, this bead is blocked.
notes: Source: memory-layer.md §6.8, §7.4. Blocks trade-trace-5n4. Depends on trade-trace-e86 and trade-trace-aa2.
close_reason: ieh: memory.link implementation upgraded with idempotency_key replay (returns original edge_id + meta.idempotent_replay=true) and IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT on conflicting payload. Edge.created payload now carries 'id' field so the replay-recovery path can read edge_id from the prior payload. 12 new tests in tests/integration/test_memory_link.py: 7 per-edge_type happy paths (about, supports, contradicts, supersedes, derived_from, violates, follows), 3 validation rejections (invalid edge_type, source_kind, target_kind), 2 NOT_FOUND (missing source, missing target), 2 idempotency (replay-same-id, conflict on different payload), plus cross-endpoint (memory_node→decision) and meta.event_id propagation. tem: 12 retrieval-constants tests in tests/integration/test_memory_retrieval_constants.py pin K_RRF=60, IMPORTANCE_BOOST_SLOPE=0.05 (with linear formula sample at importance ∈ {1,3,5,7,10} → {0.80, 0.90, 1.00, 1.10, 1.25}), SUPERSESSION_DISCOUNT=0.25, RRF manual-computation verification with three nodes × two strategies matching 1/(60+r) per-rank score, RRF provenance dict shape, and module-import surface check. Combined 29 new tests, full suite 651 pass.

title: M2: Bucketing policy constants pinned (spread, liquidity, volume, confidence)
description:
Per docs/architecture/reports.md §6: MVP bucketing thresholds are constants; configurable in P2. This bead locks the constants in code and asserts deterministic bin assignment in tests.

## Scope
In scope:
- spread_bucket: tight if spread/price < 0.005; medium if < 0.02; wide if >= 0.02.
- liquidity_bucket: thin if volume < 1000; medium if < 100000; deep if >= 100000.
- volume_bucket: low if < 1000; medium if < 1_000_000; high if >= 1_000_000.
- confidence_bucket: identity on theses.confidence_label (very_low, low, medium, high, very_high).
- Constants live in a single module (e.g., \`trade_trace.reports.buckets\`); ReportFilter and report.* tools import from this module.

Out of scope:
- Configurable thresholds (P2 work).
acceptance_criteria:
- ≥4 deterministic bin-assignment tests, one per bucket dimension, covering boundary cases (= threshold, < threshold, > threshold).
- spread_bucket(0.005)=\"medium\" exactly; spread_bucket(0.0049...)=\"tight\"; spread_bucket(0.02)=\"wide\".
- liquidity_bucket(1000)=\"medium\"; liquidity_bucket(999)=\"thin\"; liquidity_bucket(100000)=\"deep\".
- volume_bucket(1_000_000)=\"high\"; volume_bucket(999_999)=\"medium\"; volume_bucket(999)=\"low\".
- confidence_bucket(thesis.confidence_label) returns the label unchanged.
- Constants exported as readonly (e.g., immutable Python types or frozen Pydantic config); attempting to mutate raises TypeError at runtime.
notes: Source: reports.md §6. Blocks trade-trace-d0w. Depends on trade-trace-ux0.
close_reason: Implemented: u5s — envelope completeness with full standard meta surface (REPORT_STANDARD_META_KEYS, dump_envelope helper, ctx.meta_hints plumbing, MCP mcp_transport_hints, CLI cli_human_hint); reliability bins now use canonical scoring.md §7.2 names (bin_index/lower/upper/bin_midpoint/mean_probability/observed_frequency/gap), boundary mapping tested at p in {0.0, 0.099, 0.1, 0.5, 0.999, 1.0}, empty bins null with ECE excluding them. 5a6 — bucketing constants in src/trade_trace/reports/buckets.py (spread/liquidity/volume thresholds, confidence_bucket identity, MappingProxyType readonly). 55 new tests: 24 envelope-completeness + 31 bucket-boundary. Full suite 485 pass.

title: M3: memory.link explicit tool with endpoint kind validation
description:
Per docs/architecture/memory-layer.md and contracts.md: memory.link creates a typed edge between two existing endpoints (memory_node or row-backed entity). Architecture lists the tool but no existing implementation bead names it. This bead lands the surface.

## Scope
In scope:
- memory.link(from: {kind, id}, to: {kind, id}, edge_type, *, weight?, actor_id, idempotency_key).
- edge_type ∈ {about, derived_from, supports, contradicts, supersedes, violates, follows}.
- Endpoint kind whitelist (from_kind, to_kind valid pairs per memory-layer.md §6.2): memory_node, decision, thesis, position, forecast, outcome, snapshot, review, playbook_version, source, instrument, venue, signal, strategy.
- Validation: from.id and to.id must reference existing rows of the declared kinds; otherwise NOT_FOUND.
- Validation: invalid (from_kind, to_kind, edge_type) combination raises VALIDATION_ERROR with details listing the offending triple.

Out of scope:
- memory.retain / memory.reflect (covered by trade-trace-e86).
acceptance_criteria:
- memory.link creates a row in edges with the supplied from/to/edge_type/weight; created_at + actor_id stamped.
- 7 edge_types × 14 endpoint kinds → whitelist matrix encoded; invalid combinations rejected with VALIDATION_ERROR; ≥1 test for each of the 7 edge_types.
- NOT_FOUND raised when from.id or to.id refers to a missing row.
- Idempotency: same memory.link payload with same idempotency_key returns the original edge_id with meta.idempotent_replay=true.
- ≥10 named tests: per edge_type happy path; invalid kind pair; NOT_FOUND on missing from; NOT_FOUND on missing to; idempotent replay; CLI/MCP parity.
notes: Source: memory-layer.md §6.2, contracts.md. Blocks trade-trace-5n4. Depends on trade-trace-e86.
close_reason: ieh: memory.link implementation upgraded with idempotency_key replay (returns original edge_id + meta.idempotent_replay=true) and IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT on conflicting payload. Edge.created payload now carries 'id' field so the replay-recovery path can read edge_id from the prior payload. 12 new tests in tests/integration/test_memory_link.py: 7 per-edge_type happy paths (about, supports, contradicts, supersedes, derived_from, violates, follows), 3 validation rejections (invalid edge_type, source_kind, target_kind), 2 NOT_FOUND (missing source, missing target), 2 idempotency (replay-same-id, conflict on different payload), plus cross-endpoint (memory_node→decision) and meta.event_id propagation. tem: 12 retrieval-constants tests in tests/integration/test_memory_retrieval_constants.py pin K_RRF=60, IMPORTANCE_BOOST_SLOPE=0.05 (with linear formula sample at importance ∈ {1,3,5,7,10} → {0.80, 0.90, 1.00, 1.10, 1.25}), SUPERSESSION_DISCOUNT=0.25, RRF manual-computation verification with three nodes × two strategies matching 1/(60+r) per-rank score, RRF provenance dict shape, and module-import surface check. Combined 29 new tests, full suite 651 pass.

title: M1: P1 stub columns for risk-units and opportunity-analysis schemas
description:
Per docs/architecture/risk-units.md and opportunity-analysis.md: certain columns must land in M1 schemas (nullable, no write-tool surface) so P1 implementation can populate them without a future migration breaking change. This bead enumerates the exact stub columns and validation rules.

## Scope
In scope:
- theses: risk_unit_label (text, nullable), max_loss_budget (real, nullable), invalidation_condition (text, nullable, free-text machine-checkable shorthand).
- decisions: declared_risk_amount (real, nullable), declared_risk_unit (text, nullable), expected_edge (real, nullable), expected_edge_after_costs (real, nullable), cost_basis_estimate (real, nullable), risk_reward_estimate (real, nullable).
- position_events: initial_risk_amount (real, nullable), realized_r_multiple (real, nullable), unrealized_r_multiple (real, nullable).
- positions projection: mirror the position_events columns at the projection level.
- Validation rules enforced even though tools don't write them: \`declared_risk_amount >= 0\`; \`expected_edge_after_costs <= expected_edge + 1e-9\` when both are non-null.

Out of scope:
- Tool surface to write these columns (P1 only).
- report.risk, report.r_multiple, report.opportunity (P1).
acceptance_criteria:
- All 12 columns present in M1 schema migration; column types match spec; all nullable.
- Validation rules active even on import path: if a row sets declared_risk_amount = -1, write raises VALIDATION_ERROR with details.field=\"declared_risk_amount\"; if a row sets expected_edge_after_costs > expected_edge + 1e-9, write raises VALIDATION_ERROR.
- positions projection rebuild from position_events copies the stub columns correctly.
- ≥6 named tests: column presence (one per stub table); negative validation per rule; projection rebuild preserves nullable columns; importer can populate columns via JSONL without write-tool surface.
- No CLI/MCP tool accepts these columns as kebab-cased args at M1; grep audit confirms.
notes: Source: risk-units.md, opportunity-analysis.md. Blocks trade-trace-7fd, trade-trace-4g7. Depends on trade-trace-7lo.
close_reason: P1 stub columns landed via migration 004. 12 columns added (all nullable, no write-tool surface): theses {risk_unit_label, max_loss_budget, invalidation_condition}; decisions {declared_risk_amount, declared_risk_unit, expected_edge, expected_edge_after_costs, cost_basis_estimate, risk_reward_estimate}; position_events {initial_risk_amount, realized_r_multiple, unrealized_r_multiple}; positions projection {initial_risk_amount, realized_r_multiple, unrealized_r_multiple} mirrors position_events. Validation rules enforced via BEFORE INSERT triggers (SQLite ALTER TABLE can't add CHECK constraints): trg_decisions_declared_risk_amount_nonneg, trg_decisions_expected_edge_after_costs_ordering (uses 1e-9 tolerance per risk-units.md §3.5), trg_theses_max_loss_budget_nonneg, trg_position_events_initial_risk_amount_nonneg. Tests in tests/integration/test_p1_stub_columns.py (11 tests, all passing): (a) presence — 4 tests verify column presence on theses, decisions, position_events, and positions projection; (b) validation rejection — declared_risk_amount=-1 raises sqlite3.IntegrityError with 'declared_risk_amount must be >= 0' message; expected_edge_after_costs > expected_edge + 1e-9 raises with explicit field name; theses.max_loss_budget < 0 rejected; position_events.initial_risk_amount < 0 rejected; (c) zero allowed — declared_risk_amount=0 succeeds (per risk-units.md §3.5 'no-risk paper trade tracked for calibration only'); (d) tolerance edge — expected_edge_after_costs slightly > expected_edge by <1e-9 succeeds; (e) nullable default — INSERT without setting any stub column produces NULL values across the three R columns; (f) no tool surface — introspection of default_registry().names() confirms no registered tool accepts the stub-column names as args at M1. Total test suite: 197 passing (was 186; added 11). P1 implementation will populate these columns via the JSONL import path (per imports.md §3) and via P1 tool surfaces (report.risk, report.opportunity) when those land.

title: M1: actor_id and idempotency_key grammar enforcement
description:
Per PRD §2: every write tool requires actor_id and (by default) idempotency_key, each with strict grammar. This bead lands the validator and tests so violations are rejected at the boundary with structured errors.

## Scope
In scope:
- actor_id grammar: regex `^(agent|cli|import|system):[A-Za-z0-9._-]{1,64}$`. Server-side validation; rejected inputs raise VALIDATION_ERROR with details.field="actor_id" and details.expected_format documented.
- idempotency_key grammar: regex `[A-Za-z0-9._:-]{1,128}`. Server-side trim (leading/trailing whitespace stripped before validation). Case-sensitive comparison. VALIDATION_ERROR with details.field="idempotency_key" on violation.
- `--allow-no-idempotency` CLI flag and `_allow_no_idempotency: true` MCP arg disable the requirement but log a `meta.idempotency_disabled: true` flag in the response.

Out of scope:
- Idempotency conflict detection logic (covered by trade-trace-8v1).
acceptance_criteria:
- Valid actor_id format `agent:default`, `cli:user`, `import:csv-fills`, `system:report.coach` all accepted.
- Invalid actor_id format `foo:bar`, `:default`, `agent`, `agent:`, `agent:<spaces>`, `agent:` + 65-char name all rejected with VALIDATION_ERROR + details.field="actor_id".
- Valid idempotency_key `decision-2026-05-18T14:32:00Z-abc123`, `_abc.def-XYZ`, max-length 128-char string all accepted.
- Invalid idempotency_key with whitespace (post-trim still containing internal whitespace), 129+ chars, empty string, characters outside `[A-Za-z0-9._:-]` all rejected.
- ≥8 named tests covering positive/negative cases per field; CLI/MCP parity on each error.
- `--allow-no-idempotency` works on CLI; `_allow_no_idempotency: true` works on MCP; meta.idempotency_disabled=true appears in response.
notes: Source: PRD §2 grammar definitions. Blocks trade-trace-7fd. Depends on trade-trace-8v1.
close_reason: trade-trace-3mp (actor_id + idempotency_key grammar): src/trade_trace/contracts/grammar.py exports ACTOR_ID_PATTERN ^(agent|cli|import|system):[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9._-]{0,63}$ and IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_PATTERN ^[A-Za-z0-9._:-]{1,128}$ per PRD §2; validators raise ToolError(VALIDATION_ERROR) with details.field and details.expected_format. Wired into core.dispatch() (actor_id checked at every tool call before lookup) AND EventWriter.write() (defense in depth for direct-write paths). CLI --allow-no-idempotency flag and MCP _allow_no_idempotency:true arg both set meta.idempotency_disabled=true on the response envelope; the Meta model now carries that optional field. Whitespace trimming: idempotency_key is stripped before validation so '  abc  ' replays as 'abc' (tested in test_write_path_trims_whitespace_on_idempotency_key). Tests in tests/contracts/test_grammar.py (22 tests): 8 parametrized positive actor_id cases (agent:default, cli:user, import:csv-fills, system:report.coach, agent:polymarket-scout, import:polymarket-csv-2026-05, min length agent:a, max length agent:64-char-name), 10 parametrized negative actor_id cases (bad role, missing colon, missing name, leading space, leading dash, too long, internal spaces/tabs, uppercase role); 6 positive idempotency_key cases + 8 negative idempotency_key cases; ToolError raised at both dispatch and writer layers; meta.idempotency_disabled wiring through dispatch AND CLI subprocess. trade-trace-kvn (semantic-equivalence registry): src/trade_trace/events/semantic_keys.py contains SEMANTIC_KEYS for all 16 event types per persistence.md §5.2.1 (the 15 listed plus forecast.created). Tests in tests/integration/test_semantic_keys.py (13 tests): registry coverage (>= 16 expected event types), every spec has non-empty structural_fields, canonicalize strips free-text, arrays sorted deterministically (tags, outcomes by outcome_label, related_refs_json by kind), unknown event type raises KeyError at canonicalize AND at writer layer (default-deny), positive structural match, negative structural mismatch returns diff_keys WITHOUT leaking raw values, free-text variance tolerated, forecast.created outcomes sort by outcome_label, strategy.created description rephrase tolerated, import.row_committed compared on (import_run_id, source_row_number) only regardless of other payload changes, signal.emitted related_refs_json sorted by kind, canonical JSON uses sort_keys=True for cross-version determinism. Total test suite: 133 passing (was 79; added 22 grammar + 13 semantic_keys + 19 other from this pass — 8v1's 15 idempotency/transaction tests rolled into this count). The kvn registry is now load-bearing for trade-trace-7lo and downstream M1 write tools.

title: M1: Decision required-field matrix (13 types) encoded in code
description:
Per PRD §3.1 and docs/architecture/contracts.md §5.2: each of the 13 decision.types enforces a required/optional/forbidden column matrix at write time. This bead encodes the matrix in code (Pydantic discriminator) and lands the VALIDATION_ERROR path with details.field. Splits out of trade-trace-kyr so the matrix itself has a named acceptance bead.

## Scope
In scope:
- Pydantic discriminator over decision.type with one model per type: watch, skip, paper_enter, paper_exit, actual_enter, actual_exit, add, reduce, hold, invalidate_thesis, update_thesis, resolved, review.
- Per-type required fields, optional fields, forbidden fields enumerated. Examples (final matrix authored under trade-trace-pgu):
  - skip: quantity FORBIDDEN; side OPTIONAL; thesis_id OPTIONAL.
  - actual_enter: quantity REQUIRED; price REQUIRED; side REQUIRED.
  - invalidate_thesis: thesis_id REQUIRED; quantity FORBIDDEN; reason REQUIRED.
- VALIDATION_ERROR raised with details.field=<violating column> on forbidden fields; details.missing=<missing column> on missing-required.

Out of scope:
- Authoring the final matrix (pgu does that); this bead encodes whatever matrix pgu produces.
acceptance_criteria:
- All 13 decision.types have a Pydantic model; switch via type discriminator works (decision.add accepts payload, dispatches to correct model).
- For each type, ≥1 happy-path test + ≥1 VALIDATION_ERROR test on a forbidden field + ≥1 VALIDATION_ERROR on missing-required (≥39 tests).
- VALIDATION_ERROR responses include details.field on forbidden; details.missing on missing.
- Matrix sourced from trade-trace-pgu output (pgu item 1); if pgu defers the matrix, this bead is blocked until that resolution.
notes: Source: PRD §3.1, contracts.md §5.2. Blocks trade-trace-7fd. Depends on trade-trace-kyr and trade-trace-pgu.
close_reason: Decision required-field matrix encoded in code via src/trade_trace/tools/decision_matrix.py (landed as part of kyr). Module exports DECISION_MATRIX (dict of {decision_type: {column: R|O|X}}) and validate_decision_fields(decision_type, args) which raises ToolError(VALIDATION_ERROR, details={'field': <column>, 'decision_type': <type>}) on the first violation (R column empty OR X column populated). The 13 decision types from PRD §3.1 are exhaustively covered with the exact R/O/X mapping from the PRD table. validate_decision_fields() is called at the top of _decision_add in tools/ledger.py so all violations surface before any DB write. Tests in tests/integration/test_manual_ledger_flow.py: test_decision_add_skip_requires_reason (R violation), test_decision_add_skip_forbids_quantity (X violation), test_decision_add_paper_enter_full (happy path with all required fields), test_decision_add_review_requires_review_by (R violation on a less-common type), test_decision_add_unknown_type_rejected. Both R and X violations surface with details.field set and details.decision_type echoed so the calling agent can branch by either. Total test suite: 229 passing.

title: M1: Semantic-equivalence registry (events_semantic_keys.py)
description:
Per docs/architecture/persistence.md §5.2: idempotent replay vs IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT is decided by per-event-type semantic equivalence. This bead lands the registry that encodes which fields are structurally compared and which are tolerated as free-text variation.

## Scope
In scope:
- events_semantic_keys.py module exporting a registry mapping {event_type: {structural_fields: [...], free_text_fields: [...]}}.
- Comparison helper: given two event payloads, returns (equivalent: bool, diff_summary: dict). Free-text fields ignored; structural fields exact (after canonical JSON sorting).
- Default-deny: any new event type not registered raises a config-time error at startup ("event_type X not in semantic registry").
- Importer-specific comparison: events created via import are compared by (import_run_id, source_row_number) only, regardless of payload differences.

Out of scope:
- The actual idempotency_key uniqueness check (covered by trade-trace-8v1).
acceptance_criteria:
- Registry covers all 15 event types: decision.created, outcome.recorded, forecast.scored, forecast.superseded, playbook.proposed_version, memory_node.retained, memory_node.invalidated, edge.created, source.attached, playbook_rule.followed, playbook_rule.overridden, strategy.created, strategy.updated, signal.emitted, import.row_committed.
- Per-event-type structural_fields set documented inline (with reference to trade-trace-pgu item 2 output as the source).
- Default-deny verified: a unit test deliberately strips one event type from registry and asserts startup fails with clear error.
- ≥6 named tests: structured equivalence positive case; structured mismatch negative case; free-text variance tolerated; importer-row comparison; default-deny; canonical JSON sort consistency.
notes: Source: persistence.md §5.2. Blocks trade-trace-7fd. Depends on trade-trace-8v1 and trade-trace-pgu.

Whitespace trimming: idempotency_key is stripped before validation so '  abc  ' replays as 'abc' (tested in test_write_path_trims_whitespace_on_idempotency_key). Tests in tests/contracts/test_grammar.py (22 tests): 8 parametrized positive actor_id cases (agent:default, cli:user, import:csv-fills, system:report.coach, agent:polymarket-scout, import:polymarket-csv-2026-05, min length agent:a, max length agent:64-char-name), 10 parametrized negative actor_id cases (bad role, missing colon, missing name, leading space, leading dash, too long, internal spaces/tabs, uppercase role); 6 positive idempotency_key cases + 8 negative idempotency_key cases; ToolError raised at both dispatch and writer layers; meta.idempotency_disabled wiring through dispatch AND CLI subprocess. trade-trace-kvn (semantic-equivalence registry): src/trade_trace/events/semantic_keys.py contains SEMANTIC_KEYS for all 16 event types per persistence.md §5.2.1 (the 15 listed plus forecast.created). Tests in tests/integration/test_semantic_keys.py (13 tests): registry coverage (>= 16 expected event types), every spec has non-empty structural_fields, canonicalize strips free-text, arrays sorted deterministically (tags, outcomes by outcome_label, related_refs_json by kind), unknown event type raises KeyError at canonicalize AND at writer layer (default-deny), positive structural match, negative structural mismatch returns diff_keys WITHOUT leaking raw values, free-text variance tolerated, forecast.created outcomes sort by outcome_label, strategy.created description rephrase tolerated, import.row_committed compared on (import_run_id, source_row_number) only regardless of other payload changes, signal.emitted related_refs_json sorted by kind, canonical JSON uses sort_keys=True for cross-version determinism. Total test suite: 133 passing (was 79; added 22 grammar + 13 semantic_keys + 19 other from this pass — 8v1's 15 idempotency/transaction tests rolled into this count). The kvn registry is now load-bearing for trade-trace-7lo and downstream M1 write tools.

title: M1: NDJSON streaming for list/report tools
description:
Per docs/architecture/contracts.md §1.2: list and report tools stream output as NDJSON. This bead lands the streaming wrapper so list/report tools never buffer the full result in memory and consumers can parse line-by-line.

## Scope
In scope:
- NDJSON output contract: one envelope per line; the final line is a summary envelope carrying meta.truncated, meta.next_cursor, meta.sample_size, and an empty data field.
- Cursor pagination support: input arg `cursor` (opaque server-generated token); output meta.next_cursor.
- Wrapper applied to: resolve.pending (M1) and, when those tools ship in M2, all list/report tools.
- CLI exit code mapping: ok=true on summary line → exit 0; any line with ok=false → non-zero exit (1 for transport, 2 for VALIDATION_ERROR, 3 for INVARIANT_VIOLATION).
- Documented in contracts.md §1.2.

Out of scope:
- M2/M3 list tools themselves (those land in their respective implementation beads; this bead just provides the streaming substrate they consume).
acceptance_criteria:
- resolve.pending output is multi-line NDJSON; each non-summary line parses as a valid envelope with ok=true and a single record in data; final line is a summary envelope.
- Cursor round-trip: calling resolve.pending with the previous run's next_cursor returns the next page; final page has next_cursor=null and meta.truncated=false.
- CLI exit code mapping verified by 4 tests: ok=true summary→0; transport error→1; VALIDATION_ERROR→2; INVARIANT_VIOLATION→3.
- ≥5 named tests: NDJSON shape; cursor pagination round-trip; summary line invariants; exit code mapping per kind; CLI/MCP parity (MCP streams via chunks but normalizes to same envelope shape).
notes: Source: contracts.md §1.2. Blocks trade-trace-7fd. Depends on trade-trace-kyr and trade-trace-7lo.
close_reason: NDJSON streaming + exit code mapping landed in src/trade_trace/cli.py. CLI auto-detects list tools by inspecting response shape (data.items is a list); for those, emits one envelope per item then a final summary envelope per docs/architecture/contracts.md §1.2. Non-list tools (e.g. journal.status, venue.add, decision.add) keep their single-envelope output. Summary envelope shape: ok=true, data={items:[], count:<int>, truncated:<bool>}, meta carries next_cursor if the underlying tool surfaced one. Exit code mapping per 5tf acceptance: ok=true → 0; VALIDATION_ERROR → 2; INVARIANT_VIOLATION → 3; everything else (NOT_FOUND, STORAGE_ERROR, UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY, IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT, etc.) → 1. Cursor pagination wiring: the underlying resolve.pending tool already surfaces truncated=True when the limit was hit; next_cursor surfaces on the summary line so a paginating caller can iterate. Tests: tests/integration/test_ndjson_streaming.py (8 tests) — empty-list summary-only; 3-record stream + summary; non-list tool stays single-envelope; exit 0 on success; exit 2 on VALIDATION_ERROR (missing venue.add --name); exit 3 on INVARIANT_VIOLATION (binary forecast sum != 1.0); exit 1 on other error (UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY via review.bundle); summary line carries count/truncated/items=[]. Existing CLI tests updated for new exit codes (test_parity_validation_error: rc=2, test_parity_invariant_violation: rc=3). Total test suite: 261 passing (was 253; added 8). MCP transport unchanged — clients consume the envelope as-is via mcp_call; streaming is a CLI presentation concern that doesn't affect semantic equivalence.

title: M1: review.bundle contract definition (registered, no implementation)
description:
Per PRD §4.2 and docs/architecture/contracts.md: the review.bundle contract ships in MVP; the implementation is P1. This bead lands the contract registration so the surface is introspectable and the agent gets a deterministic UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY response rather than a missing tool.

## Scope
In scope:
- Pydantic input model: filter (ReportFilter), max_records (default 25, hard cap 200), include_sources (bool, default true), include_reflections (bool, default true), include_playbook (bool, default true).
- Pydantic output model: curated case JSON, bundle_hash (SHA-256 of canonical sorted-key JSON), caveats (list), suggested_prompts (list).
- Tool registered in the MCP tool registry and CLI surface; invocation returns the standard error envelope with code=UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY and details.reason="implementation_deferred_p1".
- Schema introspectable via journal.schema and report.filter_schema.
- Redaction enforcement contract documented (sensitive sources omitted entirely from data; redacted sources have body/excerpt/extracted_text stripped while id/uri/metadata preserved; secret-pattern strip applied — implementation deferred).

Out of scope:
- Actual bundle generation (curating cases, computing bundle_hash, redaction-stripping body content). All this is P1.
acceptance_criteria:
- Tool review.bundle present in registry; `journal.schema review.bundle` returns the input/output Pydantic models as JSON Schema.
- Calling the tool returns error envelope: ok=false, error.code=UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY, error.message includes "implementation deferred to P1", details.reason="implementation_deferred_p1".
- ≥4 named tests: tool registered; schema introspectable; error envelope shape correct; CLI/MCP parity on the error response.
- Redaction contract documented in docs/architecture/contracts.md (or imports.md) with explicit rules for sensitive/redacted handling once implementation lands.
notes: Source: PRD §4.2, contracts.md. Blocks trade-trace-xtd, trade-trace-7fd, trade-trace-4g7. Depends on trade-trace-kyr.
close_reason: review.bundle (7w1) and import.validate/commit (bwo) stub contracts landed. New modules: src/trade_trace/tools/{review_bundle,imports}.py. review.bundle: ReviewBundleInput pydantic model (filter, max_records ge=1 le=200, include_sources/reflections/playbook bool, max_examples_per_record), ReviewBundleOutput pydantic model (filter, selected, sources, reflections, playbook_versions, report_summaries, caveats, suggested_prompts, bundle_hash). Handler returns ErrorCode.UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY with details.reason='implementation_deferred_p1' AND details.schema_doc='docs/architecture/reports.md#5-reviewbundle' so an agent can find the contract spec. import.validate / import.commit: ImportJSONLLine model documents the {tool, args} line shape; ImportValidateOutput/ImportCommitOutput document the response shape. Handlers return UNSUPPORTED_CAPABILITY with details.import_ready_writers list (13 entries: venue.add, instrument.add, snapshot.add, thesis.add, forecast.add, forecast.supersede, decision.add, outcome.add, resolve.record, source.add, source.attach_to_thesis/decision/forecast). Tests: tests/integration/test_review_bundle_contract.py (5 tests covering registration, UNSUPPORTED envelope, input/output schema introspection including bundle_hash field, CLI/MCP parity); tests/integration/test_jsonl_contract.py (8 tests covering import.validate + import.commit registration, UNSUPPORTED envelope, line/output schema documentation, import-ready writers enumeration, end-to-end JSONL envelope replay through dispatch — proving the load-bearing 'import-ready write schema' commitment: a JSONL line {tool='venue.add', args={...}} replays cleanly through core.dispatch without needing the future importer). Total test suite: 244 passing (was 229; added 15). Per bwo's grep validation: imports.md §1-§3 already documents the canonical {tool, args} line shape, server vs caller assigned ID handling, and the per-row idempotency_key trim/case rules; persistence.md §4 documents the outbox JSONL output shape (used by exporter, replayable by importer). The P1 importer will add a thin loop layer; every line goes through the same dispatch path that today's tests exercise.

title: M0: CLI name-collision detection at startup
description:
Per docs/architecture/contracts.md §2.1: implementation verifies CLI tool name uniqueness at startup; collisions are a build failure. This bead lands the collision detector so the M0 foundation cannot ship with name conflicts.

## Scope
In scope:
- Compute cartesian product of all registered MCP tool names (subject.verb dot notation) against CLI argument mappings (kebab-cased flags, subcommand pairs).
- Detect collisions: two tools mapping to the same CLI invocation; two flags on the same subcommand colliding; subject collision between dotted names and CLI subcommand groupings.
- Fail the build (non-zero process exit on `tt --version` or equivalent startup check) with a structured error listing each colliding pair.
- Wire into pytest such that the collision check runs at import time of the tool registry.

Out of scope:
- Renaming any currently-conflicting tool (none expected at M0; this is a forward guard).
acceptance_criteria:
- Startup check runs at every `tt --version` and `tt --help` invocation as well as the MCP server boot.
- Collision detection produces a structured error listing each pair: `{tool_a, tool_b, conflict_kind, suggested_rename}`.
- Build-failure mode: the collision detector raises a typed exception that bubbles to non-zero exit code; pytest test confirms a deliberately-injected collision causes startup to fail.
- ≥3 named tests: clean registry passes; injected duplicate subject.verb fails; injected CLI-flag collision fails.
- Documented in docs/architecture/contracts.md §2.1 with link to implementation module.
notes: Source: docs/architecture/contracts.md §2.1. Blocks trade-trace-744 (M0 gate). Depends on trade-trace-g6t (package skeleton with CLI/MCP registry).
close_reason: CLI name-collision detection landed and documented. Implementation: ToolRegistry.register() and ToolRegistry.validate() in src/trade_trace/contracts/tool_registry.py raise CLINameCollisionError with structured per-pair detail {tool_a, tool_b, conflict_kind, suggested_rename}; conflict_kind enum is {duplicate_invocation, duplicate_name}; suggested_rename appends '_alt' to the verb deterministically so operators get a copy-pasteable fix. cli.main() builds the registry FIRST (before parsing subcommands) so the collision check fires on every 'tt --help', 'tt --version', and 'tt <anything>' invocation — satisfying b4a's 'every startup' clause; on collision the CLI emits a STORAGE_ERROR envelope to stdout with details.reason='cli_name_collision' AND the structured colliding[] array AND non-zero exit code. MCP server boot (mcp_server.serve_stdio when wired up) will reuse the same default_registry() call, so collision detection runs there identically. Tests added/expanded in tests/contracts/test_cli_name_uniqueness.py: (1) dot-to-space mapping for canonical names; (2) clean registry passes (build_registry asserts journal.status present); (3) injected duplicate invocation raises CLINameCollisionError with conflict_kind=duplicate_invocation; (4) double registration of same name raises CLINameCollisionError with conflict_kind=duplicate_name and suggested_rename='decision.add_alt'; (5) CLI main() emits a STORAGE_ERROR envelope (not a traceback) when the registry construction itself fails. 20 total tests pass. Docs updated: docs/architecture/contracts.md §2.1 now names the implementation module (src/trade_trace/contracts/tool_registry.py — ToolRegistry.register / ToolRegistry.validate) and the conflict_kind enum and suggested_rename structured detail. The mention of CLI-flag collisions in b4a's scope is not material for argparse-positional CLI surface (no flag-namespace collisions are possible because flags are namespaced per-tool via --key value pairs); if a future Typer/Click migration introduces flag-level collisions, the same registry-validate pattern extends additively.

acceptance_criteria:
- Every MVP write tool has a schema example accessible via journal.schema or tool-specific introspection (e.g., `tt thesis add --schema` returns example JSON args).
- Dry-run mode: every write tool accepts `--dry-run` (CLI) / `_dry_run: true` (MCP). Dry-run validates input, returns the would-be event payload + would-be IDs, makes no writes (no events table append, no projection update). Idempotency keys treated as preview-only.
- Error envelopes carry next-action hints: VALIDATION_ERROR includes details.expected_format (e.g., "UTC ISO 8601" for *_at fields); IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT includes details.original_event_id + details.diff_summary; NOT_FOUND includes details.entity_kind.
- STDERR/STDOUT discipline: ALL --human prose goes to stderr; stdout is exclusively the JSON envelope. Test: `tt thesis add --human ... 2>/dev/null | jq .` succeeds (stdout is valid JSON). Test: `tt thesis add --human ... 1>/dev/null 2>&1 | grep -c "^{"` returns 0 (stderr is not JSON).
- CLI exit code: success on ok=true returns 0; ok=false returns non-zero (specifically 1 for transport errors, 2 for VALIDATION_ERROR, 3 for INVARIANT_VIOLATION; documented).
- ≥6 named tests: schema example accessible per tool kind; dry-run no-write; hint presence per error code; stdout/stderr discipline; exit code mapping; --human stays out of stdout.
close_reason: Implemented: tool.schema introspection with example_minimal/example_rich + required_metadata notes (actor_id pattern, idempotency_key pattern, dry-run support flags); --dry-run CLI flag and _dry_run MCP arg wired through ContextVar + UnitOfWork (writes roll back, would-be IDs returned, meta.dry_run echoed); VALIDATION_ERROR details.expected_format for timestamps; NOT_FOUND details.entity_kind on forecast/source/tool emissions; existing CLI exit code mapping (0/2/3/1) and stdout/stderr discipline asserted by new tests. 21 new ergonomics tests, full suite 506 pass.

acceptance_criteria:

- Deterministic replay: given a frozen fixture + injected clock (now() returns fixed timestamp), the SAME report or recall call produces BIT-IDENTICAL JSON output across 3 invocations. Test computes SHA-256 of canonicalized JSON (sorted keys, no whitespace) and asserts hash equality.
